cards.a: Action: Break the model into smaller 'Intermediate' models and materialize them as Tables. | Error: Increasing the thread count; this will actually make the timeout more likely by competing for resources.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and only run tests for models that 'failed' last time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select state:failed'; this only runs tests, it doesn't rebuild the failed models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Variable' that is defined in another project. How?
cards.a: Action: Variables are project-scoped; you must define it in your project or pass it via CLI. | Error: Assuming 'ref()' pulls variables from the upstream project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Source code' of a macro in dbt docs. How?
cards.a: Action: dbt Core docs do not show macro source; you must view it in the Git repo or IDE. | Error: Searching dbt Explorer for 'Macro source'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build a model that uses a 'Recursive CTE'. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: You must use a 'Table' materialization (on most warehouses) as Views often struggle with recursion limits. | Error: Using View materialization.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have NOT been modified. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select !state:modified'; dbt uses the exclude flag for negative selection.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to track 'Data Quality' scores in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: This is a feature of dbt Cloud's Explorer that aggregates test pass/fail rates. | Error: Looking for a 'quality' tag in YAML.

cards.q: Scenario: Your 'Seed' load is failing because of a duplicated header in the CSV. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Clean the CSV file to ensure all header names are unique. | Error: Trying to fix this in 'dbt_project.yml'; seed configuration cannot resolve physical CSV structure issues.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that 'Finance' models are built into a specific warehouse. How?
cards.a: Action: Set the 'database' config for the finance folder in 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Changing the profile; this affects the whole run, not just one folder.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip any tests for models that didn't rebuild. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt build' runs all tests for the selected models. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-tests-on-unchanged' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to track 'GDPR' sensitive models in your project. How?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'meta' tag in the YAML (e.g. meta: {pii: true}). | Error: Using 'tags: [pii]'; 'meta' is better for structured metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific 'Snapshot' via the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt snapshot --select my_snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run --select my_snapshot'; run does not execute snapshots.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' that is only defined in one specific project. How?
cards.a: Action: Macros are project-scoped; use '{{ my_project.my_macro() }}'. | Error: Using '{{ my_macro() }}' if there's a name conflict.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build a model that uses a 'Window Function' on a 10TB table. Efficient materialization?
cards.a: Action: Incremental (if possible) or a Table; window functions on 10TB inside a View will be extremely slow. | Error: View materialization.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; sources have no parents.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that all 'Marts' models are built as Tables by default. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under the 'marts' folder path config. | Error: In the global 'models' config; this would force Staging views to be Tables too.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a 'Seed' but only if it has changed. Command?
cards.a: Action: dbt doesn't have 'seed:modified'; you must run 'dbt seed'. | Error: 'dbt seed --select state:modified'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Variable' in the 'dbt_project.yml' file. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ var("my_var") }}'. | Error: Using quotes inside the double quotes (e.g. "{{ var("my_var") }}"); causes YAML parsing error.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see which models are 'Public' in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Filter the Explorer view by 'Access' level. | Error: Looking for a 'public' tag; access is a separate property.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are NOT tagged 'pii'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^tag:pii'; dbt does not use the caret for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to generate the 'Alias' for every model. How?
cards.a: Action: Override the 'generate_alias_name' macro in your project. | Error: Editing the model config 'alias' for every file; not scalable.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Data Freshness' in the lineage graph. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: You must have run 'dbt source freshness' and be viewing the results in dbt Explorer. | Error: Assuming it's in the standard lineage.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are parents of 'fct_orders' AND children of 'stg_users'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders stg_users+'; space is UNION, comma is INTERSECTION.

cards.q: Scenario: Your 'Seed' contains a date column that dbt is loading as a string. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Set the column type to 'date' or 'timestamp' in the 'dbt_project.yml' seed config. | Error: Changing format in CSV; picky auto-detection.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Status' of every model in the last run in the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --resource-type model --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list status'; no such command exists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that all 'Staging' models are never built as Tables. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' set materialized: view for the staging path. | Error: Global config; prevents Marts from being Tables too.

cards.q: Scenario: Failing because of 'Long Query' timeout in Snowflake. Architectural fix?
cards.a: Action: Break into smaller 'Intermediate' models and materialize as Tables. | Error: Increasing thread count; competes for resources.

cards.q: Scenario: Build and only run tests for models that 'failed' last time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select state:failed'; doesn't rebuild failed models.

cards.q: Scenario: Use 'Variable' defined in another project. How?
cards.a: Action: Variables are project-scoped; define in your project or pass via CLI. | Error: Assuming 'ref()' pulls variables.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Source code' of a macro in dbt docs. How?
cards.a: Action: Core docs do not show; view in Git repo or IDE. | Error: Searching dbt Explorer for 'Macro source'.

cards.q: Scenario: Build model that uses a 'Recursive CTE'. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Must use 'Table' materialization (usually) as Views struggle with recursion limits. | Error: Using View materialization.

cards.q: Scenario: Run all models that have NOT been modified. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select !state:modified'; exclude flag for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: Track 'Data Quality' scores in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Cloud's Explorer feature aggregates test pass/fail rates. | Error: Looking for a 'quality' tag.

cards.q: Scenario: Seed load failing because of duplicated header in CSV. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Clean CSV ensuring unique header names. | Error: Fixing in dbt_project.yml.

cards.q: Scenario: Ensure 'Finance' models build into specific warehouse. How?
cards.a: Action: Set 'database' config for the finance folder in 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Changing profile; affects whole run.

cards.q: Scenario: Build and skip tests for models that didn't rebuild. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt build' runs all tests for selected models. | Error: Searching for --skip-tests flag.

cards.q: Scenario: Track 'GDPR' sensitive models. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'meta' tag in YAML. | Error: Using 'tags'; meta better for structured metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: Run specific 'Snapshot' via CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt snapshot --select my_snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run --select my_snapshot'; run doesn't execute snapshots.

cards.q: Scenario: Use 'Macro' defined in one specific project. How?
cards.a: Action: Project-scoped; use '{{ my_project.my_macro() }}'. | Error: Using '{{ my_macro() }}' if name conflict.

cards.q: Scenario: Build model with 'Window Function' on 10TB table. Efficient?
cards.a: Action: Incremental or Table; window functions on 10TB in View is extremely slow. | Error: View materialization.

cards.q: Scenario: See lineage of a Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; no parents.

cards.q: Scenario: Ensure 'Marts' built as Tables by default. Where?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_project.yml' under 'marts' folder path config. | Error: Global config.

cards.q: Scenario: Run 'Seed' but only if changed. Command?
cards.a: Action: No 'seed:modified'; run 'dbt seed'. | Error: 'dbt seed --select state:modified'.

cards.q: Scenario: Use 'Variable' in 'dbt_project.yml'. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ var("my_var") }}'. | Error: Nested quotes e.g. "{{ var("my_var") }}"; YAML parsing error.

cards.q: Scenario: See which models 'Public' in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Filter Explorer by 'Access' level. | Error: Looking for 'public' tag.

cards.q: Scenario: Run all models NOT tagged 'pii'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^tag:pii'; no caret for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: Use 'Macro' to generate 'Alias' for every model. How?
cards.a: Action: Override 'generate_alias_name' macro in project. | Error: Editing model config alias for every file.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Data Freshness' in lineage graph. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Run 'dbt source freshness' and view in Explorer. | Error: Assuming it's in standard lineage.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models parents of 'fct_orders' AND children of 'stg_users'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: Space is UNION, comma is INTERSECTION.

cards.q: Scenario: Seed 'date' column loaded as string. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Set column type to 'date' or 'timestamp' in 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Changing format in CSV.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Status' of every model in last run in CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --resource-type model --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list status'.

cards.q: Scenario: Ensure 'Staging' never built as Tables. Where?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_project.yml' set materialized: view for staging path. | Error: Global config.

cards.q: Scenario: Failing because of 'Long Query' in Snowflake. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Break into smaller 'Intermediate' models and materialize as Tables. | Error: Increasing thread count.

cards.q: Scenario: Build and only run tests for 'failed' models last time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select state:failed'.

cards.q: Scenario: Use 'Variable' in another project. How?
cards.a: Action: Project-scoped; define in yours or pass via CLI. | Error: Assuming 'ref()' pulls vars.

cards.q: Scenario: You are a Producer in a dbt Mesh setup. You need to ensure 'customer_id' is always an Integer to avoid breaking downstream projects. How do you enforce this?
cards.a: Action: Define a model 'contract' in YAML with 'data_type: integer' and 'enforced: true'. | Error: Using a 'not_null' test; tests only alert you after bad data is already written to the warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to remove a column from a public model used by other teams. You want to give them 30 days to migrate. What is the best practice?
cards.a: Action: Create a new 'version' of the model and set a 'deprecation_date' on the old version. | Error: Renaming the column and aliasing the old name to NULL; this breaks downstream logic expecting actual data types.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to show a Tableau dashboard's dependency on a dbt model in the lineage graph. How?
cards.a: Action: Define an 'Exposure' in a YAML file and list the model in 'depends_on'. | Error: Adding a comment in the SQL file; dbt metadata and lineage can only be generated from YAML definitions.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to prevent any developer from accidentally dropping the 'Production' schema during a local run. How?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the 'dev' target in 'profiles.yml' points to a unique personal schema (e.g., dbt_jsmith). | Error: Sharing 'prod' credentials for local development; this is the leading cause of accidental production data loss.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to provide a searchable 'Description' for a macro in dbt Explorer. Where is this defined?
cards.a: Action: In a 'docs' block within a '.md' file. | Error: Using a Jinja comment in the .sql file; these are for code readability and do not render in the documentation UI.

cards.q: Scenario: Your 'Finance' project needs to use the 'Orders' project's data in a Mesh setup. What file do you edit in the Finance project to enable this?
cards.a: Action: 'dependencies.yml' to list the upstream project and its production manifest. | Error: Adding it to 'packages.yml'; Mesh uses dependencies to reference live data, not raw code imports.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find 'Orphan' models that aren't used by any downstream models or dashboards. What tool is best?
cards.a: Action: Check dbt Explorer and filter for nodes with no children or Exposures. | Error: Assuming that if a model builds successfully, it is being used in the business.

cards.q: Scenario: A team is complaining that your Mesh 'Producer' model is slow. You realize they are querying a View you exposed. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Materialize the public model as a 'Table' or 'Incremental'. | Error: Keeping it as a View; views in Mesh force the Consumer to pay the compute cost of the Producer's logic.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to prevent any project from referencing your 'Intermediate' staging tables in a Mesh setup. Config?
cards.a: Action: Set 'access: private' in the model's YAML properties. | Error: Setting 'access: internal'; internal allows references within the project, but private is the most restrictive for preventing Mesh leaks.

cards.q: Scenario: You are versioning a model. Which version does dbt point to if a user writes '{{ ref('my_model') }}' without a version argument?
cards.a: Action: It points to the 'latest_version' explicitly defined in the YAML. | Error: Assuming it defaults to the highest numerical version automatically.

cards.q: Scenario: You have enforced a contract, but the build is failing even though the SQL is correct. What did you likely miss in YAML?
cards.a: Action: Ensure *every* column in the SQL output is defined in the YAML file. | Error: Forgetting that enforced contracts require 100% column coverage in the property file.

cards.q: Scenario: Your CEO asks, 'Who owns the Revenue number in this dashboard?' Where in dbt is this recorded?
cards.a: Action: The 'owner' field in the Exposure or the model's 'group' configuration. | Error: Checking Git Blame; the person who last touched the code isn't necessarily the business owner.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to include a 3-paragraph explanation of a business metric in dbt docs. How to keep YAML clean?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'doc block' in a '.md' file. | Error: Cramming long text into 'schema.yml' strings; this makes the YAML unreadable for other engineers.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to find all models containing 'email_address' for a GDPR audit. How in dbt Cloud?
cards.a: Action: Use the Search bar in dbt Explorer to filter by column name. | Error: Manual 'grep' in the IDE; this misses columns created via dynamic SQL or macros.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to automate a Slack alert when a new 'public' model is added. What artifact do you parse?
cards.a: Action: 'manifest.json' after the production build. | Error: Checking the 'models/' folder via Git; the manifest contains the final 'access' state after compilation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to standardized 'Gross Margin' logic for all BI tools. Feature?
cards.a: Action: Define it as a 'Metric' in the dbt Semantic Layer. | Error: Just creating a model 'fct_margin'; without a semantic definition, users may still re-calculate it incorrectly in BI.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using dbt Mesh. A Consumer build fails because your Producer model changed. Why didn't the Contract catch this?
cards.a: Action: Check if 'enforced: true' was set; a contract without enforcement is just documentation. | Error: Assuming a list of columns in YAML acts as a restrictive gate by itself.

cards.q: Scenario: You have two projects: Sales and Inventory. Both need a 'dim_dates' table. Governance plan?
cards.a: Action: Build 'dim_dates' in a 'Core' project and expose it as a 'public' model for both. | Error: Copy-pasting the SQL into both projects, leading to logic drift over time.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to signal if a dashboard is 'Production Ready' or 'Experimental' in dbt. Config?
cards.a: Action: Set the 'maturity' field in the Exposure definition. | Error: Using 'tags'; maturity levels are a first-class property of Exposures specifically for this purpose.

cards.q: Scenario: A team needs to use your model but wants to rename columns for their tool. Best practice?
cards.a: Action: Let them create an 'Intermediate' model in their project that aliases your public model. | Error: Changing names in your Producer model; this breaks all existing consumers.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply a 'not_null' test to every column in a table automatically. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, you must define it for each column in YAML (or use a macro to generate YAML). | Error: Thinking 'dbt build' implies column-level null checks automatically.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Model Versions'. How do you point a ref to 'v2' specifically?
cards.a: Action: '{{ ref('model_name', version=2) }}'. | Error: '{{ ref('model_name_v2') }}'; dbt uses the version argument to resolve the correct versioned node.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to hide a specific folder from dbt Explorer lineage. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, dbt shows all nodes in lineage; you can only hide 'descriptions' from the UI. | Error: Setting 'docs: {show: false}' and expecting the node to disappear from the graph.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to track 'GDPR' sensitive models in your project. Best config?
cards.a: Action: Use 'meta' tags in YAML (e.g., meta: {pii: true}). | Error: Using 'tags: [pii]'; 'meta' is better for structured metadata consumed by external security tools.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a Seed used in production. You want to ensure data types are locked. How?
cards.a: Action: Define the seed and column types in 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Relying on CSV headers; dbt auto-detection can change types if the CSV data shifts slightly.

cards.q: Scenario: What is the primary role of the 'Architect' in a dbt Mesh organization?
cards.a: Action: To define project boundaries, public interfaces, and global governance standards. | Error: To write the SQL for all departmental Marts; Mesh is about decentralizing development.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check for 'undocumented' models. What tool provides this metric out of the box?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer or the 'dbt-project-evaluator' package. | Error: Manually scanning 'manifest.json'; Explorer provides a visual dashboard for health metrics.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a Snapshot. Where?
cards.a: Action: Snapshots appear as their own nodes in dbt Explorer lineage. | Error: Assuming snapshots are 'invisible' metadata; they are fully-fledged nodes in the DAG.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' from 'dbt_utils'. First step?
cards.a: Action: Add 'dbt-labs/dbt_utils' to 'packages.yml' and run 'dbt deps'. | Error: Copy-pasting the macro code into your project; this loses the benefit of package updates.

cards.q: Scenario: A team is using your public model. You delete the model. What happens to them?
cards.a: Action: Their next build will fail during compilation. | Error: Thinking they will keep using 'cached' data; dbt validates all refs at the start of every run.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a 'Consumer' project referencing a 'Producer' project. Both use Snowflake. Does Mesh move data?
cards.a: Action: No, dbt Mesh uses cross-database/schema SQL references in the same warehouse. | Error: Thinking Mesh 'copies' data between projects; it only shares metadata and SQL paths.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to enforce that a 'Total' column matches 'Items' sum. Where is this 'governed'?
cards.a: Action: In a Singular Test or a Unit Test. | Error: In an enforced contract; contracts check schema/types, not business logic or data values.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to group models by 'Business Domain' (e.g., Marketing). Feature?
cards.a: Action: Use 'Groups' in YAML. | Error: Using 'Tags'; groups allow for formal ownership and organizational structure in dbt Explorer.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Status' of models across 5 different projects. Where?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer's Mesh view. | Error: Checking 5 separate 'dbt docs' sites; Explorer centralizes metadata from all interconnected projects.

cards.q: Scenario: You set 'persist_docs: {relation: true}'. Where does the model description appear?
cards.a: Action: In the data warehouse (e.g., as a COMMENT on the table). | Error: Only in dbt Explorer; persist_docs explicitly pushes metadata to the physical warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to reference a model in a different database. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'database' and 'schema' configs in conjunction with 'ref()'. | Error: Hardcoding 'db.schema.table' in SQL; this breaks environment-specific target resolution.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to track the 'maturity' of a model (e.g., Seed, Alpha, Beta). Config?
cards.a: Action: Use a custom 'meta' field in YAML. | Error: Using 'maturity' in a model; maturity is a specific property for Exposures, not Models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Column-level Lineage' for a specific model. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud (Explorer) is required for native column-level lineage. | Error: Expecting it in dbt Core; Core only provides model-level lineage in the docs UI.

cards.q: Scenario: A Consumer project is building. It needs the Producer's manifest. How does it get it?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud automatically fetches it from the Producer's designated 'Production' job. | Error: You must manually upload 'manifest.json' to the Consumer project's folder.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Naming Conventions' (stg_, fct_) are followed. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt-project-evaluator' to flag models that don't match prefixes. | Error: Manually checking every Pull Request; this is not scalable as the project grows.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Data Freshness' results alongside your lineage. Where?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer provides a freshness overlay on the lineage graph. | Error: Checking 'manifest.json'; the manifest doesn't store the *result* of the freshness run, only the config.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a 'Business Term' (e.g., LTV) once and use it across projects. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the dbt Semantic Layer (Metrics). | Error: Writing the definition in a 'doc block'; doc blocks provide context but don't enforce calculation logic across BI tools.

cards.q: Scenario: You are versioning a model (v1, v2). Both are live. How does dbt handle the table names?
cards.a: Action: dbt appends the version to the identifier (e.g., my_model_v1, my_model_v2). | Error: Both are named 'my_model' and dbt overwrites the one that runs last.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Downstream Impact' of a Source change. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer's lineage graph, starting at the Source node. | Error: Checking the 'Exposures' list; exposures only show the end-points, not the full intermediate impact.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to prevent any 'internal' models from being accidentally made 'public'. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Use a custom 'dbt-project-evaluator' rule to flag unexpected 'access' configs. | Error: Relying on YAML defaults; you must explicitly govern the 'access' property.

cards.q: Scenario: You are designing a Mesh. Where should 'Common Macros' live?
cards.a: Action: In a shared dbt Package installed by all projects. | Error: In the 'Core' project and shared via Mesh; Mesh shares data, not code (macros).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Query Performance' metrics in dbt Cloud. Where?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer under the 'Performance' tab for a specific model. | Error: Checking the 'run_results.json' file locally; Explorer provides historical trends, not just a single run.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Documentation' is updated for every PR. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Use a CI check that fails if a new model is missing a 'description' in YAML. | Error: Encouraging developers to 'write good docs' without an automated enforcement mechanism.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see how many dashboards are 'at risk' due to a failed model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer Lineage (Exposures nodes downstream of the failure). | Error: Checking the 'Tableau' interface; Tableau only shows that data is missing, not *why* it failed in dbt.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to generate the 'Alias' for every model. Where?
cards.a: Action: Override the 'generate_alias_name' macro in your project. | Error: Manually editing 'alias' in 500 model files; this is not scalable governance.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to restrict who can see the 'Source code' of a model in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Use Account-level RBAC groups in dbt Cloud. | Error: Setting 'access: private'; this restricts 'ref()' capability but doesn't hide metadata from authorized users.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use dbt Mesh. A producer model has 'enforced: true' for its contract. It fails. What happens to the consumer?
cards.a: Action: The consumer's build will likely skip or fail because the producer's production state was never updated. | Error: Assuming the consumer will just use the 'previous' day's version automatically without a skip status.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage-as-a-Service'. What tool does dbt provide?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer. | Error: 'dbt docs serve'; the docs site is a static artifact, whereas Explorer is a live, interconnected service.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply a 'Tag' to all models in a folder. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under the folder path. | Error: In 'schema.yml'; schema.yml is for properties, but project-wide inheritance lives in dbt_project.yml.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Column Data Types' in Explorer. Where?
cards.a: Action: Select a model and navigate to the 'Columns' tab in the details pane. | Error: Checking the SQL code; SQL doesn't show the warehouse's *actual* resolved data types.

cards.q: Scenario: Your project uses 'Snapshots'. You want to document the 'dbt_valid_from' column. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the YAML file where the snapshot is defined. | Error: In the model 'schema.yml'; snapshots have their own YAML properties and shouldn't be mixed with standard models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Finance' models are built into a specific database. How?
cards.a: Action: Set the 'database' config for the finance folder in 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Changing the 'profile' in profiles.yml; this affects the whole user, not a specific team's folder.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Cross-Project Lineage' in your local IDE. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, local IDEs only show the current project's DAG. | Error: Trying to configure 'dependencies.yml' to force it into the local graph.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find 'Public Model' documentation. Where?
cards.a: Action: In dbt Explorer, filtering by the 'Public' access level. | Error: In the Producer's Git repository; documentation should be consumed via the Explorer UI for ease of access.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Gross Margin' is never changed without approval. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Require Code Reviews on the 'metrics' YAML files. | Error: Locking the 'model' SQL; metrics are defined in YAML and can be changed without touching the SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Historical Build Times' for a model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer's 'Performance' tab. | Error: 'manifest.json'; the manifest only knows about the *structure*, not the *execution history*.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Maturity' for a model. Is there a built-in field?
cards.a: Action: No, you must use 'meta'. | Error: Trying to use 'maturity: high'; this only works for Exposures.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Dependencies' on a Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; sources have no parents, so the prefix does nothing.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Variable' in dbt_project.yml. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ var("my_var") }}'. | Error: Using nested quotes e.g. "{{ var("my_var") }}"; this results in a YAML parsing error.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to track 'Personal Identifiable Information' (PII). Tag vs Meta?
cards.a: Action: Use 'meta' for structured auditing; 'tags' for run-time selection. | Error: Using 'tags' exclusively for security auditing; tags are strings and harder to parse programmatically.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Column-level Lineage' for a Source. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, in dbt Explorer (Cloud). | Error: Expecting it in 'dbt docs generate'; the static docs do not support column-level lineage.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Marketing' models never reference 'Internal' finance tables. How?
cards.a: Action: Set finance models to 'access: internal'. | Error: Setting finance models to 'access: private'; private is for within-folder only, internal is for within-project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to automate 'Freshness' alerts to Slack. How?
cards.a: Action: Configure dbt Cloud notifications for the Freshness job. | Error: Trying to write a Jinja macro inside 'sources.yml' to send the alert; alerts are handled by the orchestrator.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Exposures' in your lineage graph. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: They must be defined in a YAML file. | Error: They are automatically detected from BI tool logs; dbt requires manual Exposure definitions.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to generate the 'Schema' for every model. How?
cards.a: Action: Override the 'generate_schema_name' macro in your project. | Error: Setting the schema manually in every model config; this is prone to error and hard to update.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Compiled SQL' for a model in Explorer. Where?
cards.a: Action: Select the model and click the 'Code' tab. | Error: Checking the 'run_results.json' download; Explorer renders it directly for ease of use.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Documentation' is never missing for Public models. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt-project-evaluator' to flag public models without descriptions. | Error: Relying on a 'not_null' test; tests are for data, not metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage' for a specific column. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Column-level lineage). | Error: Lineage Graph in dbt Core; that graph only shows model-to-model connections.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Entities' for the Semantic Layer. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'semantic_models' section of your YAML. | Error: In the 'metrics' section; entities define the joins, whereas metrics define the calculations.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Freshness' for 100 Sources. Best view?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer Freshness dashboard. | Error: Running 'dbt source freshness' locally and reading the terminal output.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to reference a 'Versioned' model. What is the syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ ref('my_model', version=1) }}'. | Error: '{{ ref('my_model_v1') }}'; dbt uses the version argument to find the specific version node.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Downstream' consumers of your public model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Cross-project lineage). | Error: 'dbt list' on your local project; local list doesn't know about other projects.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply 'RBAC' to dbt Explorer. Where?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Group permissions. | Error: Setting 'access' levels in YAML; access levels control 'ref()' ability, not UI visibility.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Table Size' in Explorer. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: You must have run 'dbt docs generate' and uploaded the catalog. | Error: Just running 'dbt run'; run does not gather warehouse-level physical metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a 'Business Definition' for 'Revenue'. Metric vs Model description?
cards.a: Action: Metric for calculation logic; Model description for context. | Error: Using model description to explain the calculation; descriptions don't enforce the logic in BI tools.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Historical Results' for a dbt Test. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (under the model's test history). | Error: 'run_results.json'; only stores the result of the *last* execution.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to prevent a model from being queried by BI tools. How?
cards.a: Action: Warehouse-level permissions (Revoke SELECT). | Error: Setting 'access: private' in dbt; dbt access levels only control 'ref()' within the dbt graph.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage' for a specific Tag. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select tag:finance+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select tag:finance'; this only lists the models, doesn't show the lineage context.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Access' in dbt_project.yml for a whole folder. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '+access: public' under the folder path. | Error: 'access: public' (without the '+'); project-level inherited configs require the plus sign prefix.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Freshness' for a specific Source table. Where in Explorer?
cards.a: Action: Select the Source node and click the 'Freshness' tab. | Error: Checking the 'General' tab; freshness has its own dedicated metadata view.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Public' models have an owner. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt-project-evaluator' to flag models without an 'owner' or 'group' config. | Error: Manually checking 'meta' fields; 'meta' is unstructured, 'group/owner' is the standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage' for an 'Intermediate' model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Project DAG). | Error: 'manifest.json'; the manifest is raw data, not a visual graph tool.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to reference a versioned model's 'latest' version. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ ref('my_model') }}'. | Error: '{{ ref('my_model', version='latest') }}'; latest is the default and shouldn't be passed as a string.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Cross-project' consumers. Possible in dbt Core?
cards.a: Action: No, Core only knows about its own project. | Error: Expecting 'dbt docs generate' to pull metadata from other repositories.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Access' for a model using an environment variable. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, but only in the 'config' block of the SQL file. | Error: Trying to use 'env_var' inside the YAML 'access' field; access levels must be static strings in YAML.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Project Health' across the entire Mesh. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer Mesh Overview. | Error: Checking individual 'run history' tabs; run history is job-specific, not project-wide.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Model Descriptions' are never empty. How?
cards.a: Action: CI test using 'dbt-project-evaluator'. | Error: Manual code review; too many models to check manually as the project scales.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage' for a model in a specific Database. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select database:my_db.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select my_db.*'; database is a specific selector prefix.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Column-level' PII tagging. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'columns' section of the model's YAML, under 'meta'. | Error: In the model's 'tags' list; tags are for the whole model, not specific columns.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Freshness' for all models. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, freshness only applies to 'Sources'. | Error: Running 'dbt source freshness' on the 'models' directory.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Consumer' projects stay updated. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Producer jobs should trigger Consumer jobs upon success. | Error: Consumers running on a separate timer; this leads to stale data in downstream projects.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Lineage' for a model that has an enforced contract. View?
cards.a: Action: Standard lineage graph; contracts don't change how lineage is displayed. | Error: Checking a separate 'Contracts' graph; dbt uses the same DAG for all models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Owner' for 500 models at once. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Models List view). | Error: Checking 500 separate YAML files manually.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Access' levels in a shared Package. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, packages can contain public models available for use by the installing project. | Error: Thinking 'access' only applies to dbt Mesh; it also governs 'ref()' behavior in standard projects.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Maturity' of an Exposure in Explorer. Where?
cards.a: Action: Select the Exposure node and check the details pane. | Error: Searching for 'Maturity' in the model details; maturity is exposure-only.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a 'Primary Key' that is unique 99% of the time, but the source occasionally has duplicates. You want the build to pass but receive a notification. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set the test severity to 'warn' in the YAML config. | Error: Deleting the test; removing the test hides the data quality issue entirely from the team.

cards.q: Scenario: Testing a 'Relationship' between 'fct_orders' and 'dim_customers'. The test fails because some orders have a 'NULL' customer_id. How do you fix the test logic?
cards.a: Action: Add a 'where: "customer_id is not null"' config to the relationship test. | Error: Assuming the test ignores NULLs; dbt treats a NULL child with no parent match as a failure unless filtered.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to validate transformation logic using 'Mock Data' in YAML instead of hitting actual warehouse tables. Which feature do you use?
cards.a: Action: Implement 'Unit Tests' (dbt v1.8+). | Error: Using 'Generic Data Tests'; data tests require the model to be physically built in the warehouse first.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to store specific rows that failed a 'unique' test so you can send them to the source system team. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --store-failures'. | Error: Manually writing a SELECT query; 'store-failures' automates the audit table creation and refreshes it every run.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing duplicates in a model that has a 'unique' test. The test is passing. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check 'run_results.json' to see if the test was actually executed. | Error: Assuming the test 'deletes' duplicates; tests only report issues, they do not transform or clean data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test that a 'status' column only contains 'active' or 'inactive'. Which test type?
cards.a: Action: Generic 'accepted_values' test. | Error: 'unique' test; a status column is expected to have many repeating values, so unique would fail immediately.

cards.q: Scenario: You need a test that compares the row count between two different models. How?
cards.a: Action: Write a 'Singular' test in the 'tests/' folder that returns rows if the counts differ. | Error: Using 'not_null'; this only checks for missing data in one column, not a comparison between tables.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test only if a specific environment variable is set. How?
cards.a: Action: Use Jinja '{% if env_var(...) %}' to wrap a 'Singular' test definition. | Error: Configuring this in 'profiles.yml'; profiles handle connections, not conditional node execution.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'unique' test fails on 'order_id'. You suspect multiple 'NULL' values are the cause. Why?
cards.a: Action: 'unique' tests in dbt consider NULL as a value; multiple NULLs trigger a failure. | Error: Thinking 'unique' ignores NULLs; only 'not_null' specifically targets them, but unique catches repetitions of them.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests'. You want to simulate a 'Source' table with 5 mock rows. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'input' and 'rows' blocks in the unit test YAML. | Error: Creating a CSV Seed; unit tests use in-memory mock data defined in YAML, not physical seeds.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test that fails if a 'date' column contains future dates. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Singular test: 'select * from my_table where my_date > current_date'. | Error: 'not_null' test; future dates are not null, so the test would never catch them.

cards.q: Scenario: You have 100 generic tests. You want to run them in parallel to save time. How?
cards.a: Action: dbt runs tests in parallel by default based on your 'threads' configuration. | Error: Searching for a '--parallel' flag; dbt is natively multi-threaded during the test task.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check for 'NULL' values in a column before it is used in a join. Where is the best place to test?
cards.a: Action: 'not_null' test on the Staging model. | Error: 'not_null' test on the Mart; by the time it reaches the Mart, the join has already produced incorrect results.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for a specific model AND all its downstream dependencies. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select my_model+'. | Error: 'dbt test --select +my_model'; the '+' prefix selects parents, meaning you'd test upstream data instead of downstream impact.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that a 'revenue' column is always positive. Which test type is most efficient?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_utils.expression_is_true' with 'expression: ">= 0"'. | Error: Writing a custom Singular test; generic expression tests are easier to maintain and reuse across columns.

cards.q: Scenario: Your project has a custom generic test named 'is_even'. Where must the logic be defined?
cards.a: Action: In the 'macros/' folder using the '{% test is_even(model, column_name) %}' syntax. | Error: In the 'tests/' folder; the 'tests/' folder is for Singular tests (.sql), while Generic tests are Macros.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only the tests that failed in the last run. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select state:failed --state path/to/artifacts'. | Error: 'dbt retry'; retry will attempt to rerun the models and the tests, not just the tests alone.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'store_failures'. Where does dbt create the audit tables by default?
cards.a: Action: In a new schema named [target_schema]_dbt_test__audit. | Error: In the same schema as your models; dbt separates audit data to avoid cluttering production schemas.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to limit a test to only check the last 3 days of data to save compute. Config?
cards.a: Action: Add a 'config: {where: "created_at >= current_date - 3"}' to the test in YAML. | Error: Filtering the model itself; you want the model to have all data, but the test to be performant.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'relationships' test is failing because of case sensitivity (e.g., 'abc' vs 'ABC'). Fix?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'Singular' test with 'upper()' or create a custom generic test macro. | Error: Thinking the 'relationships' test has a 'case_insensitive' flag; it performs a standard join.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that 'dbt build' doesn't stop if a test fails on a non-critical model. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set severity to 'warn' for that specific test. | Error: Using the '--fail-fast' flag; fail-fast does the opposite, stopping the whole run on the first error.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test a 'combination' of two columns for uniqueness. Best practice?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns'. | Error: Adding 'unique' tests to both columns separately; this only checks if each column is unique on its own.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests only on models with the tag 'hourly'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select tag:hourly'. | Error: 'dbt run --select tag:hourly'; run executes the models but does not validate the data quality.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Compiled SQL' of a failing Generic test. Where is it stored?
cards.a: Action: In 'target/compiled/[project]/tests/'. | Error: In 'target/run/'; tests are usually executed as 'select count(*)' which doesn't create a 'run' file in the same way models do.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a test to fail only if there are more than 100 errors. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set 'error_if: ">100"' in the test config. | Error: Manually writing a 'having count(*) > 100' in a singular test; using 'error_if' is the standard dbt-native way to handle thresholds.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test on a 'Source' instead of a model. Where do you define it?
cards.a: Action: In the 'sources' YAML file under the specific table name. | Error: In the model's 'schema.yml'; tests should be defined as close to the data entry point as possible.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test if a column is 'unique' but only for 'active' records. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'unique' test with a 'where: "status = 'active'"' config. | Error: Creating a separate model of just active records; this creates unnecessary DAG bloat just for a test.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'not_null' test is failing on a column that you know should have nulls. What is the correct governance action?
cards.a: Action: Remove the test or change it to a 'warn' severity if nulls are unexpected but acceptable. | Error: Leaving the test failing; failing tests in production lead to 'alert fatigue' and team members ignoring real issues.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests'. You changed the SQL logic and want to verify it before building. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select my_model --resource-type unit_test'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run executes the model against real data, which is exactly what unit tests are designed to avoid.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests defined in a specific folder. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select path:tests/my_folder'. | Error: 'dbt test --select my_folder'; selectors require the 'path:' prefix for folder-based execution.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure every model in your project has at least one test. What tool can automate this check?
cards.a: Action: The 'dbt-project-evaluator' package. | Error: Manually scrolling through 'dbt docs'; this is error-prone and not scalable for projects with 100+ models.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a singular test that is no longer needed. How do you disable it without deleting the file?
cards.a: Action: Add 'config(enabled=false)' to the top of the .sql test file. | Error: Renaming the file to '.sql.ignore'; dbt will still try to parse it unless it's properly disabled or moved out of the tests directory.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a 'Relationship' test, but the parent table is in a different database. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, provided your dbt profile has permissions for both databases and they are on the same warehouse instance. | Error: Thinking dbt tests only work within a single schema.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to capture the 'Failure Rate' of your tests over time. Where does dbt Cloud store this?
cards.a: Action: In the 'Explorer' metadata dashboard. | Error: In the 'run_results.json'; that file only stores the results of the specific run that just finished.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test that validates the 'Schema' of a model (columns and types). Which feature?
cards.a: Action: Model Contracts (enforced: true). | Error: 'Generic Data Tests'; data tests validate data values, while Contracts validate the table structure itself.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests'. Do you need to provide data for *all* columns in the mock input?
cards.a: Action: No, only the columns used by the logic being tested. | Error: Thinking you must provide a full schema; dbt unit tests are designed to be minimal and focused.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to exclude 'Singular' tests from a specific run. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude test_type:singular'. | Error: 'dbt test --select generic'; while this works, the 'exclude' approach is safer if you have other custom test types.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' inside a Singular test. Is this supported?
cards.a: Action: Yes, singular tests support full Jinja/Macro functionality. | Error: Thinking macros are only for models; they are frequently used in tests to generate dynamic 'where' clauses.

cards.q: Scenario: Your 'accepted_values' test is failing due to a trailing space in the data. Best fix?
cards.a: Action: Add 'trim(column)' to your Staging model SQL. | Error: Adding the value with a space to the YAML list; this 'hides' the data quality issue instead of cleaning it.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for only the models that have been 'modified'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select state:modified --state path/to/artifacts'. | Error: 'dbt build --select state:modified'; build would also rebuild the models, whereas 'test' only executes the validations.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a documentation 'Test Summary' for stakeholders. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Tests tab). | Error: 'manifest.json'; too technical for business stakeholders.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test if a 'JSON' column contains a specific key. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A 'Singular' test using the warehouse-specific JSON extract functions. | Error: 'accepted_values'; this test only works on standard scalar values, not nested structures.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply a 'severity: error' config to ALL tests in the project. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under the 'tests:' key. | Error: In the 'profiles.yml'; profiles do not control dbt resource configurations.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a 'unique' test to ignore '0' values because they are used as placeholders. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Add 'where: "column_name != 0"' to the test config. | Error: Filtering the whole model; you want the '0' values in the table, just not validated for uniqueness.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip tests for any 'Source' that is failing freshness. Is this automatic?
cards.a: Action: No, you must explicitly check freshness and handle the logic in your orchestrator. | Error: Assuming 'dbt build' includes a built-in freshness gate.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' in the lineage graph. Where do they appear?
cards.a: Action: By default, they are hidden in the graph; you must toggle the 'Tests' filter in the docs/Explorer UI. | Error: Thinking tests aren't part of the DAG.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to write a test that ensures no rows are lost between two joins. Which test?
cards.a: Action: A 'Singular' test comparing the row count of the source vs. the joined model. | Error: 'relationships' test; this only checks if the keys match, not if rows were filtered out during the join.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests'. You want to mock a 'Macro' used in the model. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: No, dbt unit tests mock inputs (Sources/Refs), not logic (Macros). | Error: Trying to define a 'mock macro' in the YAML file.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Generic' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:generic'. | Error: 'dbt test --select schema'; 'schema' was the old term, but 'generic' is the current standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to store test failures in a specific custom schema. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set the 'schema' config within the test block or 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Setting 'target.schema' in 'profiles.yml'; this changes where models are built, not specifically the test audit schema.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure a 'discount_amount' column is never greater than the 'original_price'. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_utils.expression_is_true' with 'expression: "discount_amount <= original_price"'. | Error: Creating a 'not_null' test; a value can be not null but still logically incorrect compared to another column.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a unit test for a model that contains a 'current_timestamp' call. How do you ensure the test is deterministic?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'overrides' or 'macros' block in the unit test to mock the timestamp to a fixed string. | Error: Running the test against the live warehouse; the test will fail intermittently as the 'actual' time moves forward.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test if a 'phone_number' column matches a specific Regex pattern. Best approach?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_expectations.expect_column_values_to_match_regex'. | Error: Writing a custom Singular test for every model; using a package like dbt_expectations standardizes regex testing across the project.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'dbt build'. A test fails on a 'Seed' file. What happens to the models that ref() that seed?
cards.a: Action: The models are 'skipped' and not executed. | Error: Thinking dbt will build the models anyway; 'dbt build' treats seeds as nodes in the DAG that can block downstream work.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests except for 'Relationship' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude test_name:relationships'. | Error: 'dbt test --exclude relationships'; you must use the 'test_name:' prefix to target the generic test type.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check that the 'sum' of orders today is within 10% of the sum of orders yesterday. Which tool?
cards.a: Action: A 'Singular' test that compares aggregates or 'dbt_expectations'. | Error: Generic 'unique' or 'not_null' tests; these cannot compare data across different time slices.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply a test to a 'Column' that is created inside a Macro. Where do you define the test?
cards.a: Action: In the YAML file for the model that *calls* the macro. | Error: Trying to define the test inside the macro code itself; dbt tests are properties of models/sources, not macros.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to verify that a 'Staging' model has the same number of rows as its 'Source'. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A Singular test selecting count difference from source and staging. | Error: 'relationships' test; relationship tests check for key existence, not row-for-row parity.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test a 'Snapshot' table for uniqueness on the 'dbt_updated_at' and 'id' columns. Where is this defined?
cards.a: Action: In the 'snapshots' directory within a YAML file. | Error: In 'models/schema.yml'; snapshots must have their properties defined in their own resource blocks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test and automatically 'Fix' the data if it fails. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: No, dbt tests are read-only validations. | Error: Assuming 'dbt build' has an 'auto-correct' feature; transformations must happen in the model SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Singular' tests that contain the word 'revenue' in the filename. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select revenue'. | Error: 'dbt test --select tag:revenue'; this only works if you manually added the tag to the test file.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a 'Unit Test' failing because of a data type mismatch in the mock data. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'cast' function or explicit types in the unit test YAML rows. | Error: Changing the model SQL to accommodate the test; the test should mirror the expected warehouse types.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that 'Developer A' cannot disable a 'Critical' test in a Pull Request. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Use 'CI Checks' and 'CODEOWNERS' to require lead approval for YAML changes. | Error: Trying to set 'permanent: true' in dbt; dbt config can always be changed by anyone with code access.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test if a 'Boolean' column has a proper distribution (e.g. at least 10% are TRUE). Tool?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_expectations.expect_column_values_to_be_in_type_list' or a custom Singular test. | Error: 'accepted_values'; this only checks if values are in the list, not their frequency or percentage.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for all models in the 'Marts' folder, including the tests of their parents. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select +marts'. | Error: 'dbt test --select marts'; this only tests the Marts, ignoring the data quality of the upstream Staging/Intermediate models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Test Coverage' by model in a single view. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Cloud) or 'dbt-project-evaluator'. | Error: 'dbt docs generate'; the standard docs site does not aggregate coverage percentages into a dashboard.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'unique' test is failing on a column with only one NULL value. Why?
cards.a: Action: A single NULL does not fail a 'unique' test; the failure means there is at least one other duplicate non-null value or another NULL. | Error: Assuming 'unique' fails on any NULL; it only fails if the NULL is repeated.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test on a 'Source' table that is a 'View' in the warehouse. Will it work?
cards.a: Action: Yes, dbt tests run against the relation regardless of whether it is a physical table or a view. | Error: Thinking 'Source Freshness' is the only thing you can run on a Source.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests'. You want to mock a 'ref()' that points to a model in a different project (Mesh). How?
cards.a: Action: Use the fully qualified name in the 'input' block: 'project_name.model_name'. | Error: Using only 'model_name'; unit tests must be able to uniquely identify the node being mocked.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build but only execute the tests, not the models. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test'. | Error: 'dbt build --test-only'; no such flag exists, 'test' is its own command.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to capture 'Test Failures' in a table that never gets overwritten. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: This requires a custom post-hook or a wrapper script; 'store-failures' overwrites the audit table on every run. | Error: Expecting '--store-failures' to keep historical logs by default.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to write a test that ensures a column contains only 'valid' email formats. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A Singular test with a 'REGEXP_LIKE' check. | Error: 'accepted_values'; impossible to list every valid email as an accepted value.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for all models EXCEPT those with the tag 'long_running'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude tag:long_running'. | Error: 'dbt test --select !tag:long_running'; dbt uses the exclude flag for negative selection in CLI.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply a test to a 'Source' table using a variable for the column name. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, use '{{ var("col_name") }}' in the YAML key for the test. | Error: Hardcoding the column name in YAML; this makes the source definition less flexible.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a test in the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select test_name --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list tests'; this only shows names, not the DAG connections.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'Unit Test' is passing in 'Dev' but failing in 'Prod'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check for 'Target-specific' logic in the model SQL (e.g. different timezones or logic). | Error: Assuming the mock data changed; unit tests use static data defined in the project, not warehouse data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and fail only if 'Critical' tests fail, but still run 'Warning' tests. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set non-critical tests to 'severity: warn'. | Error: Using 'dbt build --select severity:error'; this would skip running the warnings entirely.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to write a test that checks if a table is 'empty'. Why?
cards.a: Action: To ensure a 'deleted' or 'archived' model actually has 0 rows. | Error: Using 'not_null'; not_null checks columns, not the presence of rows.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests on 'Seeds' only. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select resource_type:seed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select seeds'; while this works if your folder is named 'seeds', resource_type is more robust.

cards.q: Scenario: Your Generic test macro is failing with a 'Relation not found' error. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check that the macro uses the 'model' argument to correctly 'ref' the table being tested. | Error: Hardcoding a table name inside a Generic test macro; Generic tests must be dynamic.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test that ensures 'start_date' is always before 'end_date' for every row. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_utils.expression_is_true' or a Singular test: 'select * from table where start_date > end_date'. | Error: Using 'relationships' test; relationships compare two different tables, not two columns in the same row.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Historical Pass Rate' for a specific test. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Cloud). | Error: 'catalog.json'; the catalog stores schema info, not run history or test results.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test that validates the 'Row count' doesn't drop by more than 20% compared to the previous run. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_expectations.expect_table_row_count_to_be_between' or a custom script. | Error: dbt core 'Generic' tests; standard generic tests only look at the *current* state of the data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a 'Relationship' test against a 'Source' that is updated via an external pipeline. Best practice?
cards.a: Action: Run 'dbt source freshness' before the test to ensure you aren't testing against stale data. | Error: Assuming the relationship test will fail if the source data is old; it only checks for key matches.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use 'Unit Testing' to test a model that uses a 'Source'. How do you mock the source?
cards.a: Action: Define the source name and mock rows in the 'given' block of the unit test. | Error: Using 'ref()' in the unit test to point to a seed; unit tests should mock the source directly.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for a single model but NOT its parents. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select my_model'. | Error: 'dbt test --select +my_model'; this would run tests for every ancestor in the DAG.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to write a test that fails if a 'String' column contains any non-numeric characters. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A Singular test using 'REGEXP_LIKE' for non-digits. | Error: 'accepted_values'; impossible to list every possible number string.

cards.q: Scenario: Your 'store-failures' audit tables are taking up too much storage. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Configure a 'TTL' or cleanup script in the warehouse for the audit schema. | Error: Disabling tests; data quality is worth the storage cost, you just need a lifecycle policy.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test only on 'Mondays'. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, use Jinja logic inside a 'Singular' test to check the date and 'select' 0 rows if it is not Monday. | Error: Trying to schedule a single test in dbt Cloud; schedules apply to jobs, not individual tests.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' for a specific 'Tag' in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Search/Filter Explorer by tag and then click the 'Tests' tab. | Error: Expecting the tags to appear on the tests automatically; tests only inherit tags from the model if configured to do so.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that a 'Percentage' column is always between 0 and 1. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_utils.expression_is_true' with 'expression: "col >= 0 and col <= 1"'. | Error: 'accepted_values'; percentages are continuous, not a discrete list.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a 'Singular' test but exclude a specific failing row from it temporarily. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Add a 'where' clause to the test SQL to filter out that specific ID. | Error: Deleting the failing row from the warehouse; dbt is the source of truth, the test should reflect reality.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'dbt build'. One test fails. Does dbt 'Roll Back' the entire build?
cards.a: Action: No, dbt keeps the tables it successfully built but stops any downstream work. | Error: Assuming dbt has 'Transactional' DAG builds; warehouse objects persist even after a build failure.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to write a test that checks for 'Silent Failures' (e.g. logic is valid but result is 0 rows). Which test?
cards.a: Action: A Singular test: 'select count(*) from my_model having count(*) = 0'. | Error: 'not_null'; not_null will pass if the table is empty because there are no rows to be null.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run tests for only the models that are 'Public' in a Mesh setup. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select access:public'. | Error: 'dbt test --select tag:public'; 'access' is a configuration property, not a tag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'SQL' executed for a Unit Test. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'target/compiled/[project]/unit_tests/'. | Error: Unit tests create 'dummy' SQL that doesn't hit your real tables, so it won't appear in standard model run logs.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a test that fails if the 'Avg Order Value' drops by 50%. Tool?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_expectations' or a custom script comparing to a 'Summary' table. | Error: 'accepted_values'; generic tests cannot perform cross-run historical comparisons.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests in the project. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run does not execute data tests or singular tests.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure every 'relationship' test in your project uses the 'warn' severity. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under 'tests: +severity: warn'. | Error: Manually editing every YAML file; this is not scalable for a large project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to test if a 'Latitude' column is between -90 and 90. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt_utils.expression_is_true'. | Error: 'not_null'; latitude can be not null but still exist on the Moon (+500).

cards.q: Scenario: Your CI job in dbt Cloud is taking 20 minutes because it builds the entire 2,000-model DAG for every PR. How do you optimize this?
cards.a: Action: Implement 'Slim CI' using 'state:modified+' and defer to the production manifest. | Error: Using 'dbt run --select tag:ci'; this requires manual tagging and often misses downstream dependencies affected by a change.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a 'Consumer' project to automatically run only after the 'Producer' project's daily job finishes successfully. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'Job Chaining' in dbt Cloud or an external orchestrator via the Administrative API. | Error: Scheduling both jobs at the same time (e.g., 2:00 AM); if the producer is delayed, the consumer builds using stale data.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to run a full refresh of your project, but only for models tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select tag:finance --full-refresh'. | Error: Running 'dbt run --full-refresh'; this rebuilds every model in the project, potentially costing thousands in warehouse credits.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a 'Source' that updates irregularly. You want dbt to fail the build if the data hasn't been updated in 24 hours. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Set 'freshness' with an 'error_after: {count: 24, period: hour}' in the source YAML. | Error: Adding a 'recency' test to the model; freshness checks run *before* the build, saving compute if data is already stale.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'dbt build' in production. A test fails on a 'Staging' model. What happens to the downstream 'Mart'?
cards.a: Action: The Mart is 'skipped' and not built. | Error: Thinking dbt will 'roll back' the Staging model; dbt leaves the failed Staging model as-is but stops the rest of the pipeline.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have changed since the last successful production run. What is required?
cards.a: Action: The 'manifest.json' from the last successful run and the 'state:modified' selector. | Error: Running 'dbt run'; without state comparison, dbt has no native way to know what changed in Git.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models EXCEPT those that are 'Snapshots'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude resource_type:snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run --exclude snapshots'; the selector must specify the 'resource_type' explicitly to be precise.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a single model including its parents and children in the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select +my_model+'. | Error: 'dbt list my_model'; this only confirms the model exists but provides no DAG context.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'State Deferral'. dbt is building a model in your 'Dev' schema but referencing a parent in 'Prod'. Is this correct?
cards.a: Action: Yes, this is the intended behavior to avoid rebuilding unchanged upstream parents in Dev. | Error: Assuming dbt is 'bugged'; deferral is a cost-saving feature designed for this exact purpose.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and stop immediately if any model or test fails. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --fail-fast'. | Error: Running 'dbt build'; by default, dbt will try to finish all independent branches of the DAG even if one branch fails.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are downstream of a changed model, but only the models tagged 'hourly'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select my_model+,tag:hourly'. | Error: 'dbt run --select my_model+ tag:hourly'; a space is an 'OR' (Union), while a comma is an 'AND' (Intersection).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to exclude 'Singular' tests from a specific CI run. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude test_type:singular'. | Error: 'dbt test --select generic'; while this works, the exclude approach is more explicit for removing specific resource types.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and only execute the tests for models that 'failed' in the previous run. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select state:failed --state path/to/artifacts'. | Error: 'dbt retry'; retry will attempt to rerun the models AND the tests, not just the tests.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to set a 'Timeout' for a dbt Cloud job to prevent it from running for 10 hours if the warehouse hangs. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the dbt Cloud Job 'Timeout' settings. | Error: In 'dbt_project.yml'; project files do not contain execution/timeout metadata for the orchestrator.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were NOT modified in the current Pull Request. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude state:modified --state path/to/manifest'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^state:modified'; dbt uses the 'exclude' flag for negation in the CLI.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a specific 'Target' named 'qa' for your production build. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --target qa'. | Error: 'dbt run --profile qa'; '--profile' selects the connection group, while '--target' selects the environment inside that group.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have the tag 'finance' OR the tag 'marketing'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select tag:finance tag:marketing' (space-separated). | Error: 'dbt run --select tag:finance,tag:marketing'; a comma would only run models that have *both* tags.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only models that have been 'modified' but NOT their children. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified+'; the '+' suffix would include every downstream model affected by the change.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip any tests for models that didn't rebuild. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt build' runs all tests for the selected models to ensure current state validity. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-tests-on-unchanged' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific 'Snapshot' via the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt snapshot --select my_snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run --select my_snapshot'; standard 'run' does not execute snapshot resources.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a Source to see what models it impacts. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; sources have no parents, so the '+' prefix does nothing.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were 'failed' last time AND are tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:failed,tag:finance'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:failed tag:finance'; the space would run all failures OR all finance models, wasting compute.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and only run tests for models that 'failed' last time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select state:failed'; 'test' only executes the validation, it doesn't rebuild the failed models first.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Slim CI'. You changed a 'Macro' used by 50 models. How many models will dbt build in the PR?
cards.a: Action: dbt will identify all 50 models as 'modified' because their compiled code has changed. | Error: Thinking dbt only builds the '.sql' file you touched; state comparison is deep.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are parents of 'fct_orders' AND children of 'stg_users'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders stg_users+'; space is UNION (either/or), comma is INTERSECTION (both).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and only run models that haven't been successfully built today. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Use 'state:failed' or 'state:modified' selection against a daily-refreshed manifest. | Error: Assuming dbt has an 'auto-skip' feature based on warehouse timestamps.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to trigger a dbt Cloud job via a Python script. Which feature do you use?
cards.a: Action: The dbt Cloud Administrative API (POST to /jobs/{job_id}/run). | Error: Trying to use 'dbt run' in the Python script; dbt Cloud jobs must be triggered via API to run on the Cloud infrastructure.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have an 'enforced' contract. Possible via selector?
cards.a: Action: Yes, using 'config.contract.enforced:true'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for tags or paths; selectors can target any model configuration value.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Singular' tests for the entire project. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:singular'. | Error: 'dbt test --select generic'; this would run the opposite (unique, not_null, etc.).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models and tests in a specific folder. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select path:models/marts'. | Error: 'dbt run --select marts'; 'run' only builds models and skips the tests in that folder.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Status' of every model in the last run in the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --resource-type model --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list status'; no such command exists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build a model only if the 'Source Freshness' check passes. Logic?
cards.a: Action: Execute 'dbt source freshness' first, and only proceed to 'dbt build' if the return code is 0. | Error: Thinking 'dbt build' includes an automatic freshness gate.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip tests for any 'Source' that is failing freshness. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, you must handle the logic in your orchestrator script (freshness then build). | Error: Searching for a '--skip-on-stale' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests in the project. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run does not execute data tests or singular tests.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a dbt job every 15 minutes. Where is this Cron schedule set?
cards.a: Action: In the dbt Cloud Job 'Triggers' settings. | Error: In 'dbt_project.yml'; project files do not contain scheduling/cron logic.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build 'Model B' which depends on 'Model A', but you ONLY want to build 'Model B'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select ModelB'. | Error: 'dbt run --select +ModelB'; the '+' prefix would force 'Model A' to build as well.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Target' to point to your 'Personal' schema for local dev. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'profiles.yml' under the 'dev' target. | Error: In 'dbt_project.yml'; project files should not contain individual developer credentials or schemas.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were NOT tagged 'pii'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^tag:pii'; dbt does not use the caret for negation in selectors.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and 'Exclude' a single specific test. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude test_name'. | Error: '--exclude model_name'; this would exclude every test AND the model itself.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a job and pass an environment variable at runtime. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --vars '{"my_var": "value"}''. | Error: Trying to set the variable in 'profiles.yml'; profiles are for connection details, not runtime variables.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models in a project that have a description. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, using 'config.description:*'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for tags or paths; metadata properties can also be used.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Generic' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:generic'. | Error: 'dbt test --select schema'; 'schema' was the old term, 'generic' is the current standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build all models that are children of a specific Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select source:raw_data.orders+'. | Error: 'dbt build --select +source:raw_data.orders'; the prefix selects parents, but sources have no parents.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip 'Staging' models if they already exist in the warehouse. Possible?
