cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt run' always attempts to build the selected models unless using 'Incremental' logic. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-if-exists' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were modified and also have the tag 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified,tag:finance'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified tag:finance'; space is Union (either/or).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests except for 'accepted_values' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude test_name:accepted_values'. | Error: 'dbt test --exclude accepted_values'; you must include the 'test_name:' prefix.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and capture the results in a JSON file. Command?
cards.a: Action: dbt does this automatically; check the 'target/run_results.json' file after the run. | Error: Trying to use a '--output json' flag on the build command.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models in the 'Intermediate' layer. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select path:models/intermediate'. | Error: 'dbt run --select intermediate'; folder-based selection requires the 'path:' prefix or '*' syntax.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build a model and its immediate parents only (1 level up). Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select 1+my_model'. | Error: 'dbt build --select +my_model'; this would build *every* upstream model in the hierarchy.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and continue even if a model fails, but stop the downstream of that model. Default behavior?
cards.a: Action: This is dbt's default behavior; only the failed node and its children stop. | Error: Thinking 'fail-fast' is the default; fail-fast stops the *entire* DAG build immediately.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models except those that use a specific Macro. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, selectors cannot target the internal code logic (Macros) of a model. | Error: Searching for 'macro:my_macro' selector.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have an 'Owner' email set in YAML. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.meta.owner:*'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for tags or paths.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific 'Singular' test. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select [test_name]'. | Error: 'dbt run --select [test_name]'; singular tests are not models and cannot be 'run'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are 'Seeds'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt seed'. | Error: 'dbt run --select resource_type:seed'; standard 'run' does not execute seed resources.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build using the manifest from 'Monday'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --state path/to/monday_manifest/'. | Error: Thinking dbt stores a history of manifests; you must manually provide the old manifest path.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip 'Unit Tests'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude resource_type:unit_test'. | Error: 'dbt build --skip-unit-tests'; no such flag exists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have been modified but exclude models with 'PII' tag. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified,^tag:pii'; dbt does not support caret negation inside the select flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were 'Skipped' in the last run. Selection?
cards.a: Action: Use 'state:failed' selection (skipped models are usually the result of an upstream failure). | Error: Searching for 'state:skipped' selector; dbt uses 'failed' to target the broken branch.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a job and pass a thread count of 1. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --threads 1'. | Error: Setting threads in 'dbt_project.yml'; thread counts should be controlled via CLI or profile for environment flexibility.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models in a project that have an enforced 'Contract'. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.contract.enforced:true'. | Error: Thinking contracts can't be used for selection.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests that are 'Generic' only. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:generic'. | Error: 'dbt test --select schema'; schema is the legacy term, generic is the modern standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have a 'description' in the YAML. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.description:*'. | Error: Thinking metadata selection isn't possible.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and capture the 'Logs' in a specific file. Command?
cards.a: Action: dbt stores logs in the 'logs/dbt.log' file by default. | Error: Trying to use a '--output-file' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models in the 'Marts' folder. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select path:models/marts'. | Error: 'dbt run --select marts'; folder-based selection requires the path prefix.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models except those with the 'long_running' tag. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:long_running'. | Error: 'dbt run --select !tag:long_running'; dbt uses exclude flag for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests for models that were 'Modified' in the current branch. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select state:modified --state path/to/manifest'. | Error: 'dbt test --select my_model'; you want to test *everything* that changed, not just one model.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and 'Fail Fast' only on tests, not models. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, '--fail-fast' applies to all node types (models, tests, seeds) in the build. | Error: Searching for a '--fail-fast-tests' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models and tests in a specific schema. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select schema:my_schema'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for tags or paths.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific 'Snapshot' via the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt snapshot --select my_snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run --select my_snapshot'; run doesn't execute snapshots.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests for only the models that are 'Public' in a Mesh setup. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select access:public'. | Error: 'dbt test --select tag:public'; 'access' is a configuration property, not a tag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'SQL' executed for a Unit Test. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'target/compiled/[project]/unit_tests/'. | Error: Unit tests create 'dummy' SQL that doesn't hit your real tables, so it won't appear in standard model run logs.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests in the project. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run does not execute data tests or singular tests.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a Source to see what models it impacts. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; sources have no parents, so the '+' prefix does nothing.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that all 'Marts' models are built as Tables by default. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under the 'marts' folder path config. | Error: In the global 'models' config; this would force Staging views to be Tables too.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a 'Seed' but only if it has changed. Command?
cards.a: Action: dbt doesn't have 'seed:modified'; you must run 'dbt seed'. | Error: 'dbt seed --select state:modified'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see which models are 'Public' in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Filter the Explorer view by 'Access' level. | Error: Looking for a 'public' tag; access is a separate property.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are NOT tagged 'pii'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^tag:pii'; no caret for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Data Freshness' in the lineage graph. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Run 'dbt source freshness' and view in Explorer. | Error: Assuming it's in standard lineage.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run models parents of 'fct_orders' AND children of 'stg_users'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: Space is UNION, comma is INTERSECTION.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Status' of every model in last run in CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --resource-type model --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list status'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that all 'Staging' models are never built as Tables. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' set materialized: view for staging path. | Error: Global config.

cards.q: Scenario: Build and only run tests for 'failed' models last time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed'. | Error: 'dbt test --select state:failed'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have NOT been modified. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select !state:modified'; exclude flag for negation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that are NOT tagged 'pii'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --exclude tag:pii'. | Error: 'dbt run --select ^tag:pii'; dbt does not use the caret for negation in selectors.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Generic' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:generic'. | Error: 'dbt test --select schema'; 'schema' was the old term, 'generic' is the current standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build all models that are children of a specific Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select source:raw_data.orders+'. | Error: 'dbt build --select +source:raw_data.orders'; the prefix selects parents, but sources have no parents.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip 'Staging' models if they already exist in the warehouse. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt run' always attempts to build the selected models unless using 'Incremental' logic. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-if-exists' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were modified and also have the tag 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified,tag:finance'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified tag:finance'; space is Union (either/or).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all tests except for 'accepted_values' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --exclude test_name:accepted_values'. | Error: 'dbt test --exclude accepted_values'; you must include the 'test_name:' prefix.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models in a project that have an enforced 'Contract'. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.contract.enforced:true'. | Error: Thinking contracts can't be used for selection.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only 'Generic' tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select test_type:generic'. | Error: 'dbt test --select schema'; schema is the legacy term, generic is the modern standard.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have a 'description' in the YAML. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.description:*'. | Error: Thinking metadata selection isn't possible.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip 'Unit Tests'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude resource_type:unit_test'. | Error: 'dbt build --skip-unit-tests'; no such flag exists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a Source to see what models it impacts. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.*+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source:raw_data.*'; sources have no parents, so the '+' prefix does nothing.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and 'Exclude' a single specific test. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude test_name'. | Error: '--exclude model_name'; this would exclude every test AND the model itself.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that were 'failed' last time AND are tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:failed,tag:finance'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:failed tag:finance'; the space would run all failures OR all finance models, wasting compute.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have the tag 'finance' OR the tag 'marketing'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select tag:finance tag:marketing' (space-separated). | Error: 'dbt run --select tag:finance,tag:marketing'; a comma would only run models that have *both* tags.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only models that have been 'modified' but NOT their children. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified+'; the '+' suffix would include every downstream model affected by the change.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip any tests for models that didn't rebuild. Is this possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt build' runs all tests for the selected models to ensure current state validity. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-tests-on-unchanged' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: A model fails with 'Relation already exists' during an incremental run on Snowflake. What is the most likely cause?
cards.a: Action: Check for a 'View' and 'Table' with the same name in the same schema. | Error: Assuming dbt automatically drops a View to make room for an Incremental Table; dbt requires a --full-refresh or manual drop to resolve the name collision.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Duplicates' in a downstream Mart, but the 'unique' test on the upstream Staging model is passing. How do you troubleshoot?
cards.a: Action: Check the 'Join' logic in the Intermediate/Mart layer for a 1-to-many fan-out. | Error: Re-running the Staging test; if the parent is unique, the error is almost certainly introduced during the join in the child model.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt Cloud job fails with 'Warehouse overloaded'. You have 32 threads configured. What is the fix?
cards.a: Action: Reduce the 'Threads' count in the dbt profile to match the warehouse's concurrency limit. | Error: Increasing the 'Timeout' setting; a longer timeout won't solve resource exhaustion and will only increase idle credit costs.

cards.q: Scenario: You added a column to a CSV Seed file, but it's not appearing in the warehouse after 'dbt run'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Run 'dbt seed'. | Error: Running 'dbt run'; run only compiles SQL models and does not re-upload or alter the physical tables created by seeds.

cards.q: Scenario: A Jinja macro is returning 'None' in the compiled SQL. How do you find the bug?
cards.a: Action: Use the '{{ log(...) }}' macro to print variable states to the console during execution. | Error: Checking 'target/compiled/'; that only shows the result of the failure, not the logic state that caused the 'None' value.

cards.q: Scenario: dbt throws a 'Cycle detected' error between 'Model A' and 'Model B'. How do you resolve this?
cards.a: Action: Identify which model's 'ref()' is unnecessary and replace it with a 'source()' or break the logic into a third model. | Error: Trying to use a hardcoded table name; while it bypasses the error, it breaks the DAG and lineage tracking.

cards.q: Scenario: A model build fails with 'Permission Denied' on a specific source table. You can query the table in your SQL editor. Why is dbt failing?
cards.a: Action: Verify that the 'Role' or 'User' defined in your dbt profile has the same permissions as your SQL editor login. | Error: Assuming dbt 'inherits' your personal permissions; dbt uses the specific credentials provided in 'profiles.yml'.

cards.q: Scenario: You changed a YAML file, and now dbt won't run due to a 'ScannerError'. How do you find the line?
cards.a: Action: Check for improper indentation or a missing colon in the YAML file mentioned in the error message. | Error: Editing the SQL code; 'ScannerError' is strictly a YAML syntax issue, not a SQL logic issue.

cards.q: Scenario: An incremental model on BigQuery is scanning 10x more data than expected. What is the primary check?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the 'is_incremental()' block contains a filter that utilizes the 'partition_by' column. | Error: Adding a 'limit' clause; limits in BigQuery do not reduce the data scan cost, only partitions do.

cards.q: Scenario: Your project builds locally but fails in dbt Cloud with 'Dependencies not found'. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Ensure all required packages are listed in 'packages.yml' and the CI job includes 'dbt deps'. | Error: Manually uploading the 'dbt_packages' folder to Git; this folder should be ignored and managed by dbt deps.

cards.q: Scenario: You see '0 records processed' in a Snapshot run, but you know the source data changed. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the 'updated_at' column in the source has actually moved forward. | Error: Assuming Snapshot 'checks' all columns by default; it only triggers if the specific 'updated_at' or 'check_cols' show a change.

cards.q: Scenario: A model fails with 'Column not found' after you renamed a column in an upstream model. How to identify all affected files?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt grep' or IDE search to find all instances of the old column name across the 'models/' folder. | Error: Running 'dbt build' and fixing them one by one; this is inefficient and risks missing logic in Ephemeral models.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'NoneType' errors when trying to access a variable. How to provide a fallback?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'default()' filter: '{{ var("my_var", default="fallback") }}'. | Error: Wrapping the variable in an 'if' block without an 'else'; this leaves the SQL syntax broken if the variable is missing.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'not_null' test is failing on a column, but 'select count(*) where col is null' returns 0. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check for empty strings ('') or whitespace that dbt/warehouse might be treating differently than a true NULL. | Error: Assuming dbt is 'hallucinating'; tests look for specific binary NULLs unless customized.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a failing Singular test. How do you see the rows that caused the failure?
cards.a: Action: Copy the SQL from 'target/compiled/[test_name].sql' and run it manually in your warehouse. | Error: Re-running 'dbt test'; dbt only returns the 'count' of failures, not the actual row data.

cards.q: Scenario: A model is materializing in the 'public' schema instead of 'analytics'. Precedence check?
cards.a: Action: Check if the 'schema' is hardcoded in the model's 'config()' block, which overrides the project YAML. | Error: Editing 'profiles.yml'; the profile sets the base, but the model config always has the final word.

cards.q: Scenario: You get a 'Tox' or environment error in a local dbt run. What is the first step?
cards.a: Action: Delete the 'target/' and 'dbt_packages/' folders and run 'dbt deps'. | Error: Reinstalling dbt entirely; most local errors are caused by stale artifacts in the local project folders.

cards.q: Scenario: An 'Epemeral' model is causing a 'Query too complex' error. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Change the materialization to 'View' or 'Table'. | Error: Trying to simplify the SQL logic; the issue is that Ephemeral models are CTEs, and nesting them too deep exceeds warehouse limits.

cards.q: Scenario: You updated a Source YAML with new columns, but 'dbt docs generate' doesn't show them. Why?
cards.a: Action: You must run the models that use that source or run a freshness check to update the catalog. | Error: Thinking 'docs generate' queries the warehouse directly for every possible source; it relies on the 'catalog.json' artifact.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Out of Memory' errors during a dbt run. Your project has 5,000 models. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Use 'partial parsing' and increase the memory allocation for the machine running dbt. | Error: Reducing the number of 'threads'; threads affect warehouse concurrency, but the OOM error happens in the dbt Python process itself.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'relationship' test is failing, but all keys exist in both tables. What is the hidden cause?
cards.a: Action: Check for 'Data Type' mismatches (e.g., String '123' vs Integer 123) that prevent a successful join. | Error: Assuming the data is missing; many warehouses allow comparison but will fail to join different types without explicit casting.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt run is 'hanging' indefinitely. No error is thrown. How to troubleshoot?
cards.a: Action: Check the warehouse 'Query History' for a blocked process or a long-running metadata lock. | Error: Restarting dbt immediately; you need to find the specific SQL statement that is causing the hang in the warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: You receive a 'Compilation Error' saying a macro could not be found, but it's in the 'macros/' folder. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the macro filename has a '.sql' extension and the macro name is spelled correctly in the model. | Error: Re-running 'dbt deps'; dbt deps is for external packages, it doesn't affect your local 'macros/' folder.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'Multiple objects found' when using 'ref()'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if you have two models with the same name in different folders. | Error: Renaming the 'file'; dbt cares about the model name defined in the SQL or config, not just the filename.

cards.q: Scenario: You see 'Compiled SQL' that looks correct, but it fails in the warehouse with a 'Syntax Error'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check for 'hidden' Jinja issues like a trailing comma generated by a loop or a missing whitespace. | Error: Trusting the IDE syntax highlighter; the highlighter doesn't know how the Jinja will resolve at runtime.

cards.q: Scenario: A model contract is failing because a column is 'Missing', but it is in the SELECT statement. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the column is being aliased differently in the SQL vs the YAML definition. | Error: Thinking dbt 'ignores' case sensitivity in contracts; contracts are often case-sensitive depending on the warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing '0 records' in an incremental model. You just did a full-refresh yesterday. Cause?
cards.a: Action: Check if the 'is_incremental()' logic is too restrictive or if the 'max(updated_at)' is ahead of today's data. | Error: Assuming the warehouse 'lost' the data; incremental logic is only as good as the 'where' clause.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a failing dbt Cloud CI job. Where is the first place to look?
cards.a: Action: The 'Run Details' log to see the specific dbt exit code and the failed SQL. | Error: Checking the 'GitHub' PR comments; GitHub only shows the pass/fail status, not the technical reason for failure.

cards.q: Scenario: A macro works in 'dbt run' but fails in 'dbt docs generate'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Use '{% if execute %}' to wrap any logic that requires querying the warehouse during compilation. | Error: Hardcoding 'ref()' calls inside logic that only runs during the docs generation phase.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Ambiguous column reference' in a joined model. Best fix?
cards.a: Action: Explicitly prefix every column with the table alias (e.g., 'orders.order_id'). | Error: Using 'SELECT *' from multiple joined tables; this is the leading cause of ambiguity in SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: A test fails in production but passes when you run it locally. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if your local 'Dev' schema has different/cleaner data than the 'Prod' warehouse. | Error: Assuming dbt code is 'broken'; tests reflect the data, and dev data is rarely a perfect mirror of prod.

cards.q: Scenario: You are hitting Snowflake 'Cloud Services' cost limits during dbt runs. How to troubleshoot?
cards.a: Action: Check for an excessive number of 'Show' or 'Describe' commands caused by high-concurrency dbt threads. | Error: Increasing the 'Warehouse' size; cloud services costs are separate from compute and are often tied to metadata operations.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt project fails to compile with 'dbt_project.yml not found'. You are in the project folder. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if you are in a sub-folder or if the file is named exactly 'dbt_project.yml' (all lowercase). | Error: Reinstalling dbt; this is a pathing or naming issue 100% of the time.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Division by zero' in a Mart. How to find the specific row?
cards.a: Action: Run a 'Singular' test: 'select * from mart where divisor_col = 0'. | Error: Checking the source data; the '0' might be introduced by a join or a null-to-zero conversion in the intermediate layer.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug the 'Selection' logic of a complex dbt command. How to preview nodes without running them?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt ls --select [your_complex_logic]'. | Error: Running 'dbt run --dry-run'; dbt does not have a native 'dry-run' that validates selection without building.

cards.q: Scenario: A model build fails because of a 'Schema change' in an incremental model. You used 'on_schema_change: fail'. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Update the model code to match the new schema and run with '--full-refresh'. | Error: Manually 'altering' the table in the warehouse; dbt should always be the source of truth for schema state.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'Invalid character' errors in your YAML. How to find the invisible character?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'Show Invisibles' mode in your IDE to check for 'tabs' instead of 'spaces'. | Error: Deleting and retyping the SQL; YAML errors are about the configuration files, not the SQL files.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt run is slow because of 'Parsing' time. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Enable 'Partial Parsing' in the dbt config or CLI. | Error: Deleting the 'target/' folder; this actually makes parsing slower as dbt has to rebuild the manifest from scratch.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Stale' data in a BI tool, but the dbt job says 'Success'. How to troubleshoot?
cards.a: Action: Check the 'Lineage' to ensure the BI tool is pointing to the final Mart, not an intermediate view. | Error: Re-running the dbt job; if the job succeeded, the data is there, the issue is the 'connection' or 'caching' in BI.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Invalid ref' error for a model you just created. Why?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the filename and the 'ref()' string match exactly, including underscores. | Error: Assuming 'ref()' uses the folder name; it only uses the model's 'name'.

cards.q: Scenario: A macro using 'run_query' is failing. How to debug the returned object?
cards.a: Action: Use '{{ log(results_variable, info=True) }}' to see the row/column structure of the result. | Error: Checking the warehouse logs; the warehouse only shows the query, not how dbt's Python logic handled the output.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'Database error: Transaction aborted' on Postgres. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check for a previous model failure in the same thread that didn't close its transaction. | Error: Increasing the memory; this is a logical 'state' error, not a hardware resource error.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'source freshness' check is failing, but the data is fresh. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the 'loaded_at_field' in YAML is pointing to a column with the wrong timezone. | Error: Assuming dbt 'knows' the current time; dbt compares the column value to the warehouse's 'current_timestamp'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all 'unused' sources in your project. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt-project-evaluator' to flag sources with no downstream refs. | Error: Checking the DAG in docs; the DAG only shows what *is* connected, not what *isn't*.

cards.q: Scenario: A model build fails with 'User does not have access to schema [X]'. You checked permissions. What else?
cards.a: Action: Check if dbt is trying to build into a 'Custom Schema' (e.g., analytics_finance) that hasn't been created yet. | Error: Granting more permissions to the 'Raw' schema; the error is about the 'Target' schema.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Arithmetic overflow' in a Mart. How to debug?
cards.a: Action: Identify the column causing the issue and use 'CAST(col as BIGINT)' or equivalent. | Error: Trying to fix with a dbt test; tests only happen after the data is successfully written.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Historical' error trends for a specific model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Explorer (Run History). | Error: 'run_results.json'; this file is ephemeral and is overwritten by every new run.

cards.q: Scenario: You added a package to 'packages.yml', but its macros are still not found. Why?
cards.a: Action: You must run 'dbt deps' to physically download the package files into your project. | Error: Restarting the IDE; the IDE can't find files that haven't been downloaded yet.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Column 'X' is ambiguous' in a model using 'dbt_utils.star'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'except' argument in 'dbt_utils.star' to remove the duplicate column name from the star. | Error: Manually listing 50 columns; the whole point of 'star' is to avoid manual lists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a 'Singular' test that only fails in CI. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'state:failed' selection in a local environment pointed at the CI target. | Error: Guessing based on the code; logic that fails in CI usually fails due to 'data' differences in the CI warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt build fails with 'Snapshot id not found'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if someone manually deleted rows from the Snapshot table in the warehouse. | Error: Running 'dbt clean'; 'clean' doesn't affect the warehouse tables, only local files.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Too many open files' during a dbt run. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Increase the 'ulimit' on the machine running dbt or reduce the number of threads. | Error: Deleting the 'target/' folder; the issue is concurrent file access by dbt's multi-threaded processes.

cards.q: Scenario: A model is materializing as a 'View' but should be a 'Table' per dbt_project.yml. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if there is a 'config()' block inside the .sql file overriding the YAML. | Error: Assuming 'dbt_project.yml' has the highest precedence; the .sql file itself always wins.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'KeyError' in a dbt run. What is the usual cause?
cards.a: Action: dbt is trying to access a dictionary key (like a tag or config) that doesn't exist. | Error: Checking the SQL syntax; 'KeyError' is a Python/Jinja metadata error.

cards.q: Scenario: You see 'Compiled SQL' using the 'dev' schema in your CI job. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the CI job is missing the '--target prod' flag or if the 'default' target in profiles is 'dev'. | Error: Thinking dbt 'knows' it is in CI; you must explicitly tell it which target to use.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Character limit exceeded' in a dbt column description. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'doc block' in an '.md' file to handle long descriptions. | Error: Trying to wrap the string in YAML; YAML has limits, but doc blocks are handled as separate files.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'not_null' test fails, but the data looks correct. How to investigate?
cards.a: Action: Check for 'invisible' characters like null bytes or non-breaking spaces. | Error: Assuming the test is 'wrong'; tests do not lie about the binary state of the data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug the 'Performance' of a specific macro. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'dbt-profiler' package or look at the 'compile' time in the logs. | Error: Checking the warehouse query time; the warehouse doesn't know about macro execution, only the final SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing '0 models found' when running dbt. You have 100 models. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if your 'dbt_project.yml' has the correct 'model-paths' configured. | Error: Re-running 'dbt deps'; 'deps' handles external code, but your local models are managed by 'model-paths'.

cards.q: Scenario: A model is slow because of a 'Recrusive CTE'. How to troubleshoot performance?
cards.a: Action: Run the compiled SQL with 'EXPLAIN' in the warehouse to find the bottleneck. | Error: Changing the materialization to 'Ephemeral'; this makes recursion even harder for the warehouse to optimize.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'invalid identifier' for a column you just added to a Source. Why?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the source table in the warehouse actually has that column; dbt doesn't 'create' source columns.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'unique' test is failing, but there are no duplicate rows in the table. How?
cards.a: Action: The test is only on one column; check if that specific column has duplicates, even if the rest of the row is different. | Error: Thinking 'unique' means the 'whole row' is unique; it only applies to the specific column(s) tested.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Lineage' of a test in the CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select [test_name] --output json'. | Error: 'dbt list tests'; this only shows names, not the DAG connections.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using 'Unit Tests' and the test is failing with 'SQL Error'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check the 'target/compiled/[unit_test].sql'; the error is usually a syntax mistake in your mock data or the model's logic. | Error: Thinking the unit test is hitting the real warehouse data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug why 'Partial Parsing' was skipped. Where?
cards.a: Action: Look for the 'Partial parsing not enabled' or 'Partial parsing out of sync' message in the dbt terminal logs. | Error: Checking 'dbt_project.yml'; partial parsing is a run-time behavior, not just a static config.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Table not found' for a model that was just built. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if the model is being built into a different 'Schema' than what you are querying in your SQL editor. | Error: Running 'dbt clean'; the table is in the warehouse, not your local project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all 'Singular' tests that are currently disabled. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt ls --select test_type:singular --state path/to/manifest' (if using state comparison). | Error: Manually opening every .sql file in 'tests/'.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt Cloud job is 'Queued' for an hour. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if you have reached your 'Slot' or 'Concurrency' limit in the dbt Cloud account settings. | Error: Restarting the warehouse; the delay is in dbt Cloud's scheduler, not the warehouse compute.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'None' values in a 'Source Freshness' report. Why?
cards.a: Action: The freshness command couldn't find the 'loaded_at_field' or the table is empty. | Error: Thinking the data is 'infinitely fresh'; 'None' means the check couldn't be performed.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a 'Macro' that generates YAML. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'dbt run-operation' command to execute the macro and print the result to the console. | Error: Calling the macro in a model; models expect SQL, not YAML, and will fail to compile.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Deadlock detected' in a dbt run. What is the architectural fix?
cards.a: Action: Reduce the number of 'threads' to minimize concurrent DDL operations on the same schema. | Error: Changing models to 'View'; views also require metadata locks in many warehouses.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Catalog' of your warehouse without running dbt docs. Where?
cards.a: Action: Query the 'information_schema.columns' directly in your warehouse. | Error: Checking 'manifest.json'; the manifest only has dbt's view of the project, not the warehouse's physical state.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'relationships' test fails, but you know the data is there. What's the hidden fix?
cards.a: Action: Check for trailing spaces or hidden characters in the keys using 'trim()' in your staging models. | Error: Manually 'inserting' missing keys into the parent table; fix the data pipeline, not the symptom.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Unexpected keyword' in a Jinja block. How to troubleshoot?
cards.a: Action: Check if you are using a Python-only function that isn't supported in Jinja's restricted sandbox. | Error: Checking the SQL syntax; 'Unexpected keyword' is a Jinja compilation error.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug the 'Selection' of models for a CI job. Command?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt ls --select state:modified --state path/to/prod/manifest'. | Error: Running 'dbt build'; you only want to 'list' the nodes to verify your selector logic.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'profile not found' when running dbt. You have a profiles.yml. Why?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the 'profile:' name in 'dbt_project.yml' matches the profile name in 'profiles.yml' exactly. | Error: Checking the warehouse credentials; the error is about the 'connection' between the project and the profile.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find the 'slowest' model in your project. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Sort the models in dbt Explorer by 'Average Execution Time'. | Error: Checking 'logs/dbt.log'; the log is a stream of text and is very hard to use for aggregate performance analysis.

cards.q: Scenario: You see '0 tests passed, 0 failed' after running 'dbt test'. You have 50 tests. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check your '--select' flag; you likely selected a model that has no tests defined. | Error: Assuming the tests 'deleted' themselves; selectors are the primary reason for 'empty' test runs.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Diff' between local and production data. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'dbt-audit-helper' package and the 'compare_relations' macro. | Error: Manually scrolling through two SQL editors; 'audit-helper' automates the comparison and provides a percentage match.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt job is slow because it's fetching the 'Manifest' from S3/Cloud. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the environment is using 'Partial Parsing' to avoid a full manifest rebuild every time. | Error: Moving dbt to a 'larger machine'; the bottleneck is network latency and parsing, not CPU/RAM.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Invalid datetime' in a Mart. How to debug?
cards.a: Action: Run a Singular test checking for '0000-00-00' or other invalid date strings in the source. | Error: Using 'not_null'; an invalid date is often not NULL, it's just garbage data that the warehouse can't parse.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a 'Macro' that uses 'graph' object. How?
cards.a: Action: Use '{{ log(graph.nodes.values() | list, info=True) }}' to see the metadata available for all models. | Error: Looking in the 'target/manifest.json' while the run is active; the manifest is only written *after* the run finishes.

cards.q: Scenario: You see 'Compiled SQL' using 'database_a' instead of 'database_b'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if 'database_a' is hardcoded in the model config or 'dbt_project.yml'. | Error: Changing the profile; the profile target is the *fallback*, but explicit config always wins.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Dependencies' for a model that was just deleted. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, if you have a copy of the previous 'manifest.json', you can use it to see what *was* dependent on it. | Error: Using 'dbt ls'; the current project no longer contains the model, so dbt doesn't know its history.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'Jinja error: expected token 'end of print statement'. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Check for a missing closing brace '}}' or an extra opening brace in the SQL. | Error: Checking the SQL syntax; this is a Jinja delimiter error.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' for a Source in dbt docs. Where?
cards.a: Action: Navigate to the 'Sources' section in the docs sidebar and select the table. | Error: Searching in the 'Models' section; source tests are attached to the source node, not the downstream models.

cards.q: Scenario: A 'not_null' test is failing, but you only care if the column is null for 'Premium' customers. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Add 'config: {where: "customer_type = 'Premium'"}' to the test in YAML. | Error: Creating a custom Singular test; adding a 'where' clause to a generic test is cleaner and built-in.

cards.q: Scenario: You are hitting BigQuery 'concurrent slot' limits. fix?
cards.a: Action: Reduce the number of 'threads' in your dbt profile. | Error: Switching to 'Ephemeral' models; Ephemeral models are CTEs and don't reduce the number of slots used by the parent query.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug the 'Freshness' of a specific Source table. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt source freshness --select source:[source_name].[table_name]'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run doesn't gather or report on freshness metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Identifier too long' in a warehouse. Architect's fix?
cards.a: Action: Use 'alias:' in the model config to provide a shorter physical name for the table. | Error: Renaming the '.sql' file; some warehouses have limits that are shorter than a nested dbt folder path.

cards.q: Scenario: You see '0 tests found' for a model that has tests defined in YAML. Why?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the model name in the YAML (under the 'models:' key) matches the SQL filename exactly. | Error: Assuming dbt 'guesses' the link based on folder proximity; the name must match.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Historical Pass/Fail' rates for a dbt Cloud job. Where?
cards.a: Action: Job 'Run History' page in dbt Cloud. | Error: 'Explorer'; Explorer shows the *current health* of the project, while Run History shows the *performance of a job* over time.

cards.q: Scenario: You are getting 'Circular reference' with a model that refs a Snapshot of itself. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Break the loop by creating an 'Intermediate' model that acts as the source for the snapshot. | Error: Using 'source()' to point to the snapshot; this hides the lineage but still causes a circular DDL dependency.

cards.q: Scenario: A model is slow. You suspect 'Broadcasting' in the join. How to verify?
cards.a: Action: View the 'Query Plan' in the warehouse to see the join type and data movement. | Error: Changing the materialization to 'Table'; this won't change the underlying SQL join efficiency.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug a failing PR in 'Slim CI'. How to see the models dbt selected?
cards.a: Action: Download the 'manifest.json' and 'run_results.json' artifacts from the CI run. | Error: Checking the dbt Cloud IDE; the IDE shows your *current* branch code, not the *result* of the CI run.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing '0 models found' after setting 'model-paths' to a new folder. Why?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the new folder is relative to the 'dbt_project.yml' file and contains .sql files. | Error: Restarting the warehouse; this is a local pathing/parsing issue.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' for an Ephemeral model. Where?
cards.a: Action: You cannot test Ephemeral models directly; you must test the 'downstream' models that consume them. | Error: Defining tests in YAML for an Ephemeral model; dbt will throw an error or skip them.

cards.q: Scenario: A model is materializing in 'dev' but should be in 'prod'. You are using '--target prod'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check if your 'profiles.yml' has the 'prod' target correctly defined and pointing to the right schema. | Error: Assuming dbt uses the 'active' target in the IDE; you must pass the flag explicitly in the CLI.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to debug 'Jinja' logic without running a whole model. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'dbt compile' command and check the 'target/compiled/' folder. | Error: Running 'dbt build'; you only want to see the *text generation*, not execute the SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Deadlock' during 'dbt seed'. fix?
cards.a: Action: Set threads to 1 for the seed run or ensure seeds don't share a schema with models running concurrently. | Error: Splitting the seed into 10 smaller files; this actually *increases* the number of concurrent DDL locks.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to generate a dynamic list of columns to exclude from a 'SELECT *' statement in a Mart. Which utility macro is the best practice?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt_utils.star(from=ref(...), except=[...])'. | Error: Writing a custom Jinja loop to fetch column names from the warehouse; dbt_utils is pre-optimized and handles warehouse-specific quoting automatically.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific piece of SQL *only* if the project is running against the 'Prod' target. How do you implement this check?
cards.a: Action: Use '{% if target.name == 'prod' %}'. | Error: Hardcoding the database name in the SQL; this makes the model non-portable and breaks developer isolation in personal schemas.

cards.q: Scenario: You are creating a macro that needs to work on both BigQuery and Snowflake for string concatenation. How do you handle the different syntax?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'adapter.dispatch' pattern to define 'concat' macros for each adapter. | Error: Using '||' or 'CONCAT()'; these are syntax-specific and will cause a compilation error on at least one of the two platforms.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to check if a table already exists in the warehouse before attempting a 'DROP' in a macro. Which object is most reliable?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'adapter' object (e.g., 'adapter.get_relation(...)'). | Error: Querying 'information_schema' manually via 'run_query'; the adapter object is faster and abstracts away warehouse-specific schema naming.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to strip whitespace from the start and end of a Jinja block to keep the compiled SQL clean for logs. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: Use '{%- ... -%}' with dashes on both sides of the percentage signs. | Error: Using standard '{% ... %}'; this leaves multiple newlines and spaces in the final SQL, making it harder to debug in warehouse history.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to iterate over a list of regions ('US', 'EU', 'APAC') to create separate columns in a model. How?
cards.a: Action: Use a '{% for region in ['US', 'EU', 'APAC'] %}' loop. | Error: Hardcoding 'select case when region = 'US'...' for every line; this is repetitive and violates the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle.

cards.q: Scenario: Your macro uses 'run_query' to get a list of IDs. It fails during 'dbt compile' but works during 'dbt run'. Why?
cards.a: Action: Wrap the logic in an '{% if execute %}' block. | Error: Assuming 'run_query' works during the compilation phase; dbt only connects to the warehouse during the 'execution' phase.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Variable' defined in 'dbt_project.yml' inside your model. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ var("my_variable") }}'. | Error: '{{ my_variable }}'; Jinja variables must be accessed via the 'var' or 'env_var' functions if they are defined in project config.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to access the 'Tag' of a model from within a macro. Which object contains this metadata?
cards.a: Action: The 'model' or 'graph' object (e.g., 'model.config.tags'). | Error: Querying the 'information_schema'; dbt tags only exist in the dbt manifest, not in the physical warehouse tables.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that calculates 'Profit Margin'. Where should this file be saved?
cards.a: Action: In the 'macros/' directory with a '.sql' extension. | Error: In the 'models/' directory; models must return a result set (SELECT), whereas macros are reusable logic blocks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to join two strings together in Jinja. What is the operator?
cards.a: Action: Use the tilde '~' (e.g., 'string1 ~ string2'). | Error: Using the plus '+' sign; in Jinja, '+' is for mathematical addition, whereas '~' forces string concatenation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all models in the project that have a specific 'meta' property using Jinja. Which object do you iterate over?
cards.a: Action: 'graph.nodes.values()'. | Error: 'model.config'; the 'model' object only has access to the *current* model's metadata, not the entire project's.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a 'Global' variable that can be overridden by a command-line argument. How?
cards.a: Action: Define it in 'vars' in 'dbt_project.yml' and access with '{{ var("name") }}'. | Error: Hardcoding it in a macro; macros are harder to override at runtime than project variables.

cards.q: Scenario: Your macro needs to return a 'List' of column names from a specific table. Which adapter method do you use?
cards.a: Action: 'adapter.get_columns_in_relation(ref('my_model'))'. | Error: 'run_query("show columns...")'; the adapter method is more portable and returns a structured list of column objects.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to print a debug message to the terminal during a dbt run. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ log("my message", info=True) }}'. | Error: '{{ print("my message") }}'; Jinja does not have a native 'print' function that pipes to the dbt CLI console.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a specific dbt Package is installed from within a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: Check for the package name in the 'graph.nodes' or use the 'adapter.dispatch' check. | Error: Assuming 'ref()' will work; ref() only works for models, not for verifying package installation.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to escape a reserved keyword (like 'order') in a dynamic SELECT statement. Which macro helps?
cards.a: Action: 'adapter.quote("order")'. | Error: Manually adding double quotes like '"order"'; this will break if you switch to a warehouse like BigQuery that uses backticks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to convert a Jinja string to all uppercase. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_string | upper }}'. | Error: Using 'upper(my_string)'; Jinja uses pipe syntax for most string transformations (filters).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that only runs on Snowflake. How do you guard it?
cards.a: Action: '{% if target.type == 'snowflake' %}'. | Error: '{% if adapter == 'snowflake' %}'; the 'adapter' is a Python object, while 'target.type' is the string name of the warehouse.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'database' name of a specific source. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ source('my_source', 'my_table').database }}'. | Error: Hardcoding 'raw_prod_db'; if the source database changes in the YAML, your macro will break.

cards.q: Scenario: You are using a loop to generate SQL. How do you prevent a trailing comma on the last item?
cards.a: Action: Use '{% if not loop.last %},{% endif %}'. | Error: Putting the comma outside the loop; this results in a comma before the first item or after every single item.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'invocation_id' in a post-hook for auditing. Why?
cards.a: Action: To uniquely identify the specific dbt run that executed the model. | Error: Using 'run_started_at'; the start time might be the same for multiple runs, but the 'invocation_id' is a unique UUID.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a macro from 'dbt_utils' but your project has its own macro with the same name. How to specify?
cards.a: Action: '{{ dbt_utils.star(...) }}'. | Error: '{{ star(...) }}'; without the package prefix, dbt will prioritize the local project macro over the package macro.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get a count of all models in your project from a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: '{{ graph.nodes.values() | selectattr("resource_type", "equalto", "model") | list | length }}'. | Error: Trying to query the 'information_schema'; the warehouse doesn't know about dbt 'models', only tables.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to set a default value for an environment variable. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ env_var("MY_VAR", "default_value") }}'. | Error: '{{ env_var("MY_VAR") or "default_value" }}'; the env_var function expects the default as its second argument.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that executes a DDL statement directly. Which macro do you call?
cards.a: Action: 'run_query(my_ddl_sql)'. | Error: 'adapter.execute(my_ddl_sql)'; while 'adapter.execute' works, 'run_query' is a higher-level wrapper that is generally preferred.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a 'ref()' is valid before using it in a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'graph.nodes' to see if the model exists in the manifest. | Error: Wrapped the ref in a 'try/except'; Jinja doesn't support standard Python-style error handling blocks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Current Timestamp' from dbt, not the warehouse. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ run_started_at }}'. | Error: '{{ current_timestamp() }}'; current_timestamp is a macro that generates warehouse SQL, whereas 'run_started_at' is a dbt-native object.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' as a default value for a variable. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, project variables must be static values (Strings, Ints, Lists). | Error: Trying to put '{{ my_macro() }}' in the 'dbt_project.yml' vars section.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find the 'Primary Key' of a model from its YAML configuration in a macro. Path?
cards.a: Action: 'model.config.meta.primary_key' (assuming it was defined in 'meta'). | Error: 'model.primary_key'; dbt does not have a first-class 'primary_key' attribute on model objects.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to generate a series of UNION ALL statements dynamically. Loop syntax?
cards.a: Action: Iterate over a list and use '{% if not loop.last %} UNION ALL {% endif %}'. | Error: Hardcoding 'UNION ALL' after every select; the final query will have a syntax error due to a trailing 'UNION ALL'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'Package Name' from within a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'project_name'. | Error: 'package_name'; in dbt context, 'project_name' refers to the name of the project/package where the macro resides.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Compiled SQL' for a macro. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'target/compiled/' folder for a *model* that calls the macro. | Error: Looking in 'target/compiled/macros/'; dbt doesn't compile macros individually, only when they are called by models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to pass a 'List' as a variable from the CLI. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --vars '{"my_list": ["a", "b"]}''. | Error: 'dbt run --vars 'my_list=a,b''; CLI variables must be valid JSON strings to be parsed as complex types like lists.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a model has a specific 'Tag' before applying logic in a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: '{% if "my_tag" in model.config.tags %}'. | Error: '{% if model.tags == "my_tag" %}'; tags are always returned as a list, so you must check for inclusion.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that calculates the 'Diff' between two timestamps. Why use a macro?
cards.a: Action: To handle the different 'DATEDIFF' syntax between Snowflake and BigQuery. | Error: Writing 'timestamp_diff' directly in the SQL; this will fail if the warehouse is changed.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Conditional' block to choose between two different SELECT statements. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{% if ... %} SELECT A {% else %} SELECT B {% endif %}'. | Error: Putting the 'if' block *inside* the SELECT list; Jinja should wrap the entire SQL block for structural changes.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'Original SQL' (uncompiled) of a model from a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.raw_code'. | Error: 'model.compiled_code'; raw_code contains the Jinja, while compiled_code contains the final SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that returns a 'Relation' object. Why?
cards.a: Action: To allow dbt to correctly identify the database/schema/table for use in an 'adapter' call. | Error: Returning a string like 'my_db.my_schema.my_table'; strings don't have the metadata needed for adapter methods.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use 'Comments' in Jinja that don't appear in the compiled SQL. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{# my comment #}'. | Error: '-- my comment'; double-dash comments are SQL-level and will appear in the compiled SQL and warehouse logs.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Thread ID' currently executing the model. Variable?
cards.a: Action: dbt does not expose the thread ID to Jinja. | Error: Trying to find a 'thread.id' variable for auditing.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a macro is being called from a 'Test' vs a 'Model'. How?
cards.a: Action: Check 'model.resource_type'. | Error: Checking 'target.name'; target.name tells you the environment, not the type of dbt resource being built.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that generates a 'Drop Table' statement but *only* in the 'Dev' environment. How?
cards.a: Action: '{% if target.name == 'dev' %} DROP TABLE... {% endif %}'. | Error: Running the macro without a guard; this could accidentally drop production tables.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to convert a 'Result' object from 'run_query' into a list of dictionaries. Filter?
cards.a: Action: Use 'results.table.to_dicts()'. | Error: Manually looping through 'results.columns' to build a dictionary; the 'to_dicts()' method is a built-in convenience.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Variable' in the 'dbt_project.yml' file. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: You must use the 'var' function; you cannot use 'ref' or 'source' in the project YAML. | Error: Trying to use '{{ ref('my_model') }}' to set a project-level variable.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Schema' of a model from within a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.schema'. | Error: 'model.config.schema'; model.schema returns the *final* resolved schema, which might differ from the config if an alias macro is used.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'indent' a block of SQL generated by a macro. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ generated_sql | indent(width=4) }}'. | Error: Manually adding spaces to every line in the macro; the 'indent' filter is cleaner and handles multiple lines.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if the current warehouse is BigQuery. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{% if target.type == 'bigquery' %}'. | Error: '{% if adapter == 'bigquery' %}'; remember, 'adapter' is a Python class instance, not a string.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a macro to generate the 'Unique Key' for an incremental model. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the model's 'config()' block: 'unique_key=my_macro()'. | Error: In the middle of the SQL; the unique_key must be part of the configuration metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Jinja' version dbt is using. How?
cards.a: Action: dbt does not expose the Jinja version directly, but it is typically tied to the dbt Core version. | Error: Trying to run '{{ jinja_version }}'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure a macro returns a 'String' even if the input is an Integer. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_int | string }}'. | Error: Assuming Jinja handles this automatically in SQL; explicit casting prevents 'NoneType' or 'Int' errors in concatenation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'Search Path' from your dbt profile in a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: dbt does not expose the warehouse 'search path' to Jinja. | Error: Trying to find 'target.search_path'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to determine which 'Source' to use. Logic?
cards.a: Action: '{{ source(my_macro(), 'my_table') }}'. | Error: Trying to put 'source' *inside* the macro; the source function should wrap the macro call for clarity.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get a list of all 'Tags' used in the entire project. How?
cards.a: Action: Loop through 'graph.nodes.values()' and collect 'config.tags'. | Error: Checking the warehouse; warehouse objects don't store dbt tags.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a 'Post-Hook' that runs a macro. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: 'config(post_hook="{{ my_macro() }}")'. | Error: 'config(post_hook=my_macro())'; hooks must be passed as strings containing the Jinja expression.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a 'Column' exists in the source table before selecting it. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'adapter.get_columns_in_relation' and check the column list. | Error: Using 'SELECT *'; this is unsafe and could pull unwanted or breaking data.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Call Stack' of macros during a failure. How?
cards.a: Action: dbt does not provide a macro call stack; you must use 'log' statements to trace the execution. | Error: Looking for a 'traceback' in the compiled SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to convert a 'List' to a comma-separated string for a 'WHERE IN' clause. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_list | join(", ") }}'. | Error: Manually looping through the list; the 'join' filter is the built-in way to handle list-to-string conversion.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if dbt is running in 'Full Refresh' mode from a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: '{% if flags.FULL_REFRESH %}'. | Error: '{% if full_refresh %}'; 'full_refresh' is a config, whereas 'flags.FULL_REFRESH' is the actual CLI state.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Model Name' from within a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.name'. | Error: 'model.identifier'; identifier is the physical table name, which might be different if an alias is used.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a macro that generates a 'Date Spine'. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: A loop that generates multiple SELECT statements joined by 'UNION ALL'. | Error: Writing one long SELECT with 365 hardcoded dates; this is not maintainable.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'Project ID' for Google BigQuery in a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'target.project'. | Error: 'target.database'; in BigQuery, 'project' is the top-level identifier, not database.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure a 'Macro' doesn't produce any output text unless called. How?
cards.a: Action: Ensure all logic is wrapped inside the '{% macro %}' block and avoid text outside it. | Error: Putting comments outside the macro block; these will be injected into every model that imports the file.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Alias' of a model. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.alias'. | Error: 'model.name'; remember, name is the dbt filename, alias is the physical warehouse table name.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a 'Variable' is even or odd. Logic?
cards.a: Action: '{% if my_int % 2 == 0 %}'. | Error: Using Python's 'is_even()'; Jinja uses standard mathematical operators for these checks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access 'Environmental Variables' on a machine without dbt. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'env_var' is a dbt-specific Jinja function. | Error: Trying to use 'env_var' in a raw .sql script.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'SQL' of a 'Macro' itself. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, macros are not nodes in the graph with a 'raw_code' property. | Error: Trying to find 'graph.macros.my_macro.raw_code'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to determine the 'Materialization' of a model. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, 'materialized=my_macro()'. | Error: Putting the macro inside quotes like 'materialized="{{ my_macro() }}"'; this will pass the string, not execute the logic.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Schema' of a Source. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: '{{ source('my_source', 'my_table').schema }}'. | Error: Hardcoding 'raw_schema'; if the YAML changes, the macro breaks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if dbt is running in 'CI' mode. Logic?
cards.a: Action: '{% if target.name == 'ci' %}' (assuming you named your CI target 'ci'). | Error: '{% if flags.CI %}'; dbt does not have a native 'CI' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all 'Tests' associated with a specific model in a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: Loop through 'graph.nodes' and filter for 'resource_type: test' and 'attached_node'. | Error: Checking the model's 'config'; tests are separate nodes in the graph, not properties of the model.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to generate a 'Case When' statement. Logic?
cards.a: Action: Loop through a dictionary of 'key: value' pairs and generate the 'WHEN' clauses. | Error: Writing 50 'WHEN' lines manually; macros are meant to automate these repetitive SQL structures.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'Warehouse' name (e.g. 'compute_wh'). Property?
cards.a: Action: 'target.warehouse'. | Error: 'target.name'; name is the dbt profile target name, not the physical warehouse resource.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Count' of items in a Jinja list. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_list | length }}'. | Error: '{{ my_list.count() }}'; count is a Python method, whereas 'length' is the standard Jinja filter.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure a macro 'returns' a value like a function. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: Macros in dbt don't have a 'return' statement; they simply output text. | Error: Trying to use '{% return my_val %}'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Lineage' (parents) of the current model in a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.depends_on.nodes'. | Error: 'model.refs'; depends_on is the actual list of unique node IDs in the manifest.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a 'Variable' is defined before using it. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{% if my_var is defined %}'. | Error: '{% if my_var %}'; if the variable is defined but False, this check will fail incorrectly.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use 'Regex' in a Jinja string replacement. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, Jinja's 'replace' filter only handles literal strings. | Error: Trying to use '{{ my_string | replace(regex, "") }}'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to access the 'dbt version' from a macro. Variable?
cards.a: Action: '{{ dbt_version }}'. | Error: '{{ version }}'; dbt_version is the specific global variable for the Core version.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to generate a 'Where' clause. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the macro handles 'None' or empty inputs to avoid 'WHERE AND...' syntax errors. | Error: Returning an empty string; if the macro is followed by an 'AND', the SQL will break.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all 'Exposures' in the project. Object?
cards.a: Action: 'graph.exposures.values()'. | Error: 'graph.nodes'; nodes usually only include models, seeds, and snapshots.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Database' of the current target. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'target.database'. | Error: 'target.project' (on Snowflake); on Snowflake it's database, on BigQuery it's project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to define a 'Column Alias'. Logic?
cards.a: Action: 'select col as {{ my_macro("col") }}'. | Error: 'select col as my_macro("col")'; macros must be wrapped in curly braces to be executed.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a model is an 'Incremental' build from a macro. How?
cards.a: Action: '{{ is_incremental() }}'. | Error: '{% if model.config.materialized == "incremental" %}'; this checks the config, but doesn't check if the table actually exists and can be updated.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Full Path' of a model file. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.original_file_path'. | Error: 'model.path'; path is the relative path from the models folder, whereas original_file_path is the full project path.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'Sort' a list in Jinja. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_list | sort }}'. | Error: '{{ my_list.sort() }}'; remember, Jinja uses pipes for almost all list/string operations.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Compiled SQL' for a Singular Test. Where?
cards.a: Action: 'target/compiled/[project]/tests/'. | Error: 'target/run/tests/'; tests don't have run artifacts in the same way models do.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to create a 'User' in the warehouse. Command?
cards.a: Action: Use 'dbt run-operation my_create_user_macro'. | Error: Putting the macro call in a model; models should only perform data transformations, not DDL administration.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Description' of a model from a macro. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.description'. | Error: 'model.config.description'; descriptions are nodes properties, not config properties.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if dbt is running in 'Debug' mode. Logic?
cards.a: Action: '{% if flags.DEBUG %}'. | Error: '{% if debug %}'; remember to use the 'flags' object for CLI-level states.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Profile' name being used. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'target.profile_name'. | Error: 'target.name'; name is the *target* (e.g. dev), profile_name is the *group* (e.g. snowflake_prod).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'Capitalize' only the first letter of a string. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{{ my_string | capitalize }}'. | Error: '{{ my_string | upper }}'; upper will capitalize every single letter.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Arguments' passed to a macro during failure. How?
cards.a: Action: Use '{{ log(...) }}' at the start of the macro to print all input arguments. | Error: Checking the compiled SQL; arguments aren't visible in the output unless they are explicitly selected.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Project Name' of the root project. Variable?
cards.a: Action: '{{ project_name }}'. | Error: '{{ package_name }}'; project_name always refers to the current executing project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Macro' to determine the 'Column Order' of a table. Logic?
cards.a: Action: A list of column names and a loop to generate the 'SELECT' list. | Error: 'SELECT *'; this relies on the source order, which dbt cannot guarantee.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to get the 'Schema' of a model's 'Alias'. Property?
cards.a: Action: 'model.schema'. | Error: 'model.alias.schema'; alias is just a string, it doesn't have its own schema property.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if a 'Variable' is a List or a String. Filter?
cards.a: Action: '{% if my_var is iterable and my_var is not string %}'. | Error: '{% if my_var is list %}'; Jinja doesn't have a native 'is list' test (dbt does in some versions, but 'iterable' is safer).

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use 'Jinja' to calculate a mathematical value for a column. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: 'select {{ 1 + 1 }} as two'. | Error: 'select 1 + 1 as two'; the second version is calculated by the warehouse, the first is calculated by dbt during compilation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to find all 'Seeds' in the project. Object?
cards.a: Action: Loop through 'graph.nodes' and filter for 'resource_type: seed'. | Error: 'graph.seeds'; seeds are stored under the 'nodes' key in the manifest.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Jinja' errors in the dbt terminal. What is the usual cause?
cards.a: Action: A syntax mistake like a missing 'endif' or a misspelled macro name. | Error: A warehouse-level 'Syntax Error'; if Jinja fails, the warehouse never even receives the query.

cards.q: Scenario: Your CI job builds 500 models every PR, taking 30 minutes. How do you optimize this?
cards.a: Action: Use 'state:modified+' and defer to the production manifest. | Error: Using 'dbt run --select tag:ci'; manual tagging misses downstream dependencies affected by logic changes.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to use an API Key in a model without committing it to Git. How?
cards.a: Action: Define a dbt Cloud Environment Variable (DBT_ENV_...) and use '{{ env_var(...) }}'. | Error: Putting the key in 'dbt_project.yml' vars; this exposes the secret to everyone with repo access.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a 'Marketing' job to trigger only after 'Core' finishes. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Use 'Job Chaining' in dbt Cloud Job settings. | Error: Scheduling both at the same time; if the first job is delayed, the second runs on stale data.

cards.q: Scenario: You see 'Model not found' in CI for a parent model you didn't touch. Why?
cards.a: Action: Enable 'Defer to Production' in CI job settings. | Error: Adding the parent model to the PR manually; this wastes compute and pollutes the branch code.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a 'Full Refresh' on Sundays, but standard 'Incremental' daily. How?
cards.a: Action: Create two separate dbt Cloud jobs with different schedules and CLI commands. | Error: Writing Jinja 'if' logic to check the day; managing execution at the job level is cleaner and more visible.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to rotate the DB password used by dbt Cloud. Where is this updated?
cards.a: Action: In dbt Cloud 'Credentials' under the Environment settings. | Error: Updating 'profiles.yml' in Git; dbt Cloud ignores local profiles in favor of UI-managed credentials.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to block a PR merge if a dbt test fails. How?
cards.a: Action: Set dbt Cloud CI as a 'Required Check' in your Git provider settings. | Error: Relying on developers to manually check the dbt UI; without a block, bad data will eventually reach prod.

cards.q: Scenario: A dbt Cloud job fails with 'Git Clone Failed'. First check?
cards.a: Action: Verify the PAT or Deploy Key permissions in your Git provider. | Error: Re-running the job immediately; Git auth errors require credential updates, not just retries.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run only models that 'Failed' in the previous run. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select state:failed --state path/to/artifacts'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run won't include the tests that might have been the reason for the failure.

cards.q: Scenario: You want 'Dev' to build into 'dev_schema' and 'Prod' into 'prod_schema'. How?
cards.a: Action: Create separate 'Environments' in dbt Cloud and set the target schema for each. | Error: Hardcoding schema names in SQL; this prevents the code from being portable across targets.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to trigger a dbt job from Airflow. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the dbt Cloud Administrative API. | Error: Using 'dbt run' in a BashOperator; dbt Cloud jobs must be triggered via API to run on Cloud workers.

cards.q: Scenario: BI tool shows stale data but dbt job says 'Success'. Troubleshooting?
cards.a: Action: Check 'Source Freshness' logs; the run might have finished but used data that was already old. | Error: Assuming 'Success' means 'Live'; success only means the SQL executed without errors.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build and skip 'Unit Tests' to save CI time. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude resource_type:unit_test'. | Error: 'dbt build --skip-unit-tests'; this flag does not exist in the dbt CLI.

cards.q: Scenario: Ensure 'Marts' only build if 'Staging' tests pass. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run does not execute tests, allowing Marts to build even if upstream data is corrupted.

cards.q: Scenario: Hitting concurrency limits in Snowflake. How to limit dbt Cloud?
cards.a: Action: Reduce the 'Threads' setting in the Environment connection. | Error: Scheduling fewer jobs; a single job with high threads can still hit the limit.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Compiled SQL' for a production failure. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the Cloud 'Run History' details, under the 'Logs' or 'Artifacts' tab. | Error: Checking local 'target/' files; local artifacts do not reflect production run states.

cards.q: Scenario: Run a job using a 'Custom Branch' for testing. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Change the 'Git Branch' in the Job settings UI. | Error: Merging to 'main' just to test in dbt Cloud; this risks breaking production code prematurely.

cards.q: Scenario: Receive Slack alerts only on 'Failure'. How?
cards.a: Action: Configure 'Notifications' in Job settings for 'On Failure'. | Error: Setting 'On Run' alerts; this causes alert fatigue when successful jobs flood the channel.

cards.q: Scenario: Moving Core to Cloud. What happens to local 'profiles.yml'?
cards.a: Action: Stop using it for Cloud; Cloud manages connections via the UI/Environments. | Error: Committing 'profiles.yml' to Git; this is a security risk and ignored by dbt Cloud anyway.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models tagged 'finance' that were modified. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified,tag:finance'. | Error: Using a space instead of a comma; space is 'OR', comma is 'AND' (Intersection).

cards.q: Scenario: Perform a 'Blue/Green' deployment swap. Process?
cards.a: Action: Use a final 'run-operation' in the job to rename the staging schema to prod. | Error: Manually renaming tables in the warehouse; this is prone to error and timing issues.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run 500 tests in parallel. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Increase 'Threads' in the connection settings. | Error: Searching for a '--parallel' flag; dbt is multi-threaded by default based on thread count.

cards.q: Scenario: Build model only if yesterday's build was a success. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'state:failed' selection against the previous manifest to identify the need for rebuilds. | Error: Searching for a 'persist_status' config in YAML.

cards.q: Scenario: Run dbt Cloud on an 'Isolated' network. Feature?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud 'Private Link'. | Error: Manually whitelisting IPs; hard to maintain and less secure for enterprise data.

cards.q: Scenario: See which user triggered a run. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'Run Details' sidebar in dbt Cloud. | Error: Checking warehouse logs; they usually only show the Service Account name.

cards.q: Scenario: Fail the entire job immediately if one model fails. Flag?
cards.a: Action: '--fail-fast'. | Error: Thinking this is default; default behavior is to finish all independent DAG branches.

cards.q: Scenario: Use different dbt 'versions' for different jobs. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'Environment' settings dropdown in dbt Cloud. | Error: Setting 'require-dbt-version' in YAML; this restricts the run but doesn't change the environment's version.

cards.q: Scenario: Run all models in the 'Intermediate' folder. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select path:models/intermediate'. | Error: 'dbt run --select intermediate'; folder paths require the 'path:' prefix or glob syntax.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to build only models that have been 'modified' but NOT their children. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified'. | Error: 'dbt run --select state:modified+'; the '+' suffix adds all downstream children.

cards.q: Scenario: Capture 'Freshness' metrics for BI tools. Step?
cards.a: Action: Add a 'dbt source freshness' step to the Cloud job before the build. | Error: Assuming 'dbt build' checks freshness automatically; it is a separate command.

cards.q: Scenario: PR merge takes 1 hour due to docs generation. Optimization?
cards.a: Action: Disable 'Generate Docs' for CI jobs; documentation is only critical for production runs. | Error: Reducing the number of models; docs generation is a metadata task that scales with project size.

cards.q: Scenario: Run only models with 'Enforced Contracts'. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select config.contract.enforced:true'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for tags or paths.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a specific 'Singular' test. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt test --select [test_name]'. | Error: 'dbt run'; singular tests are not models and cannot be 'run'.

cards.q: Scenario: Run a job every 15 minutes. How?
cards.a: Action: Set the 'Interval' or 'Cron' trigger in the Job settings. | Error: Scheduling logic in 'dbt_project.yml'; project files only contain transformation logic.

cards.q: Scenario: You need a personal schema (dbt_jsmith) for local dev. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'profiles.yml' under the 'dev' target. | Error: In 'dbt_project.yml'; project files should not store developer-specific schema names.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models that were 'Skipped' in the last run. Selection?
