cards.a: Action: Use 'state:failed' selection; skipped models are children of failed models. | Error: Searching for 'state:skipped'; 'failed' is the correct selector for the broken DAG path.

cards.q: Scenario: Pass an environment variable at runtime via CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --vars '{"my_var": "value"}''. | Error: Setting the variable in 'profiles.yml'; profiles are for connection info, not model variables.

cards.q: Scenario: Build all models children of a specific Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select source:raw_data.orders+'. | Error: 'dbt build --select +source:...'; the prefix selects parents, and sources have no parents.

cards.q: Scenario: Skip Staging models if they already exist in the warehouse. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, 'dbt run' always builds unless using 'Incremental' logic. | Error: Searching for a '--skip-if-exists' flag.

cards.q: Scenario: Run all models modified AND tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified,tag:finance'. | Error: Space instead of comma; space is 'Union', comma is 'Intersection'.

cards.q: Scenario: See which models are 'Public' in dbt Explorer. How?
cards.a: Action: Filter the Explorer list by 'Access' level. | Error: Looking for a 'public' tag; access is a separate config property.

cards.q: Scenario: PR merge blocks because of 'Docs Generate' failure. Why?
cards.a: Action: Check for invalid doc blocks or Markdown syntax in the '.md' files. | Error: Troubleshooting the SQL; doc failures are usually metadata/YAML related.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run all models that have a 'description' in YAML. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select config.description:*'. | Error: Thinking selectors only work for physical attributes like tags.

cards.q: Scenario: Build a model but skip its tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select my_model'. | Error: 'dbt build --skip-tests'; build includes tests by design, use 'run' to bypass.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run seeds, models, and tests in one go. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build'. | Error: 'dbt run'; run skips seeds and tests.

cards.q: Scenario: Ensure 'Staging' models always run as Views. Where?
cards.a: Action: In 'dbt_project.yml' under the staging path: '+materialized: view'. | Error: Setting it in every .sql file; folder-level config is the best practice for governance.

cards.q: Scenario: See historical pass/fail rates for a Job. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Job 'Run History' page in dbt Cloud. | Error: 'Explorer'; Explorer shows current project health, while Run History is job-specific.

cards.q: Scenario: Run a specific 'Snapshot' via CLI. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt snapshot --select my_snapshot'. | Error: 'dbt run'; standard run ignores snapshot resources.

cards.q: Scenario: Track 'GDPR' sensitive models. How?
cards.a: Action: Use a 'meta' tag in YAML (meta: {pii: true}). | Error: Using 'tags'; meta is better for structured metadata for external tools.

cards.q: Scenario: PR merge is slow due to 'Parsing'. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Enable 'Partial Parsing' in dbt Cloud settings. | Error: Deleting the 'target/' folder; this forces a full re-parse every time.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' for a Source in docs. Where?
cards.a: Action: Navigate to 'Sources' in the sidebar and select the table. | Error: Searching 'Models'; source tests are attached to the source node.

cards.q: Scenario: Trigger a job only when a specific file changes in Git. How?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Slim CI (state:modified) handles this automatically by comparing the manifest. | Error: Manually writing custom Git hooks.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use 'v2' of a versioned model in CI. Command?
cards.a: Action: '{{ ref('model_name', version=2) }}'. | Error: '{{ ref('model_name_v2') }}'; dbt uses the version argument for resolution.

cards.q: Scenario: Use a specific dbt version for one specific job. Where?
cards.a: Action: Job settings in dbt Cloud UI. | Error: 'dbt_project.yml'; YAML only restricts versions, it doesn't provision them.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Lineage' of a Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:raw_data.orders+'. | Error: 'dbt ls --select +source...'; the prefix is for parents, sources have none.

cards.q: Scenario: Set a 'Timeout' for a Cloud job. Why?
cards.a: Action: To prevent wasting compute credits if a warehouse query hangs indefinitely. | Error: To speed up the dbt run; timeouts stop jobs, they don't optimize them.

cards.q: Scenario: PR merge blocks on 'authentication' error. Why?
cards.a: Action: The dbt Cloud CI user/role lacks permissions in the CI schema. | Error: Checking the developer's personal warehouse permissions.

cards.q: Scenario: Exclude 'Snapshots' from build. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude resource_type:snapshot'. | Error: '--exclude snapshots'; resource type must be specified.

cards.q: Scenario: Run only models modified in the PR. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified --state path/to/manifest'. | Error: 'dbt run'; this runs everything, wasting compute.

cards.q: Scenario: You see 'Duplicates' in prod but CI tests passed. Why?
cards.a: Action: CI only tested modified models; the duplicate was introduced in a downstream join not modified in the PR. | Error: Assuming CI is a 'full' data proof.

cards.q: Scenario: See 'Data Quality' scores in Cloud. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer dashboards. | Error: Reading 'run_results.json' manually.

cards.q: Scenario: Move from Core to Cloud.profiles.yml?
cards.a: Action: Cloud handles connections in the UI; stop using local profiles.yml. | Error: Committing the file to Git.

cards.q: Scenario: Trigger job via external tool. API?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Administrative API v2. | Error: Bash commands in Airflow.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models parents of 'fct_orders' AND children of 'stg_users'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: Space instead of comma.

cards.q: Scenario: Slack alert on 'Failure' only. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Notification settings in Job UI for 'On Failure'. | Error: Webhooks for 'On Run'.

cards.q: Scenario: Rotate DB password in Cloud. Where?
cards.a: Action: Environment Credentials section. | Error: profiles.yml in Git.

cards.q: Scenario: Block PR merge on test fail. How?
cards.a: Action: Set dbt CI as a 'Required Check' in Git provider. | Error: Manual dashboard checks.

cards.q: Scenario: Git Clone Failed. First check?
cards.a: Action: PAT or Deploy Key permissions. | Error: Re-running immediately.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models modified OR tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified tag:finance' (space-separated). | Error: Comma-separated (which is AND).

cards.q: Scenario: See which user triggered a run. Where?
cards.a: Action: Run Details sidebar in Cloud UI. | Error: Warehouse Query History.

cards.q: Scenario: Fail job immediately on first error. Flag?
cards.a: Action: '--fail-fast'. | Error: Thinking it's default behavior.

cards.q: Scenario: Use dbt Cloud on 'Isolated' network. Feature?
cards.a: Action: Private Link. | Error: IP whitelisting.

cards.q: Scenario: Docs Generate slow on PR. Optimization?
cards.a: Action: Disable for CI jobs. | Error: Reducing model count.

cards.q: Scenario: Build and skip Unit Tests. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --exclude resource_type:unit_test'. | Error: Searching for --skip flag.

cards.q: Scenario: Run only models with Enforced Contracts. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select config.contract.enforced:true'. | Error: Thinking selection only works on tags.

cards.q: Scenario: Stale data alert in BI but dbt job Success. Why?
cards.a: Action: Source Freshness check; the data was old before dbt even started. | Error: Assuming SQL success means data is current.

cards.q: Scenario: Run job every 15 mins. Trigger?
cards.a: Action: Interval/Cron trigger in Job UI. | Error: dbt_project.yml.

cards.q: Scenario: Personal schema for local dev. Target?
cards.a: Action: profiles.yml 'dev' target. | Error: dbt_project.yml.

cards.q: Scenario: Build Marts only if Staging tests pass. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build'. | Error: 'dbt run'.

cards.q: Scenario: PR merge blocks on Auth error. Cause?
cards.a: Action: Cloud CI user lacks CI schema permissions. | Error: Checking dev personal permissions.

cards.q: Scenario: Run only modified models. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified --state path'. | Error: 'dbt run'.

cards.q: Scenario: Deferral building in Dev but ref() prod. Correct?
cards.a: Action: Yes, cost-saving behavior. | Error: Assuming it's a bug.

cards.q: Scenario: Run build using Monday manifest. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --state path/to/monday'. | Error: Assuming dbt stores manifest history natively.

cards.q: Scenario: Standardize Staging views in prod. Where?
cards.a: Action: dbt_project.yml path config. | Error: config() in each .sql file.

cards.q: Scenario: See which user triggered a run. Where?
cards.a: Action: Cloud UI Run Details. | Error: Warehouse logs.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models in specific schema. Selection?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select schema:name'. | Error: Path selection only.

cards.q: Scenario: Build all models children of Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select source:name+'. | Error: Prefix select.

cards.q: Scenario: PR taking long due to Parsing. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Enable Partial Parsing. | Error: Deleting target/ folder.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models parents of fct_orders AND children of stg_users. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select +fct_orders,stg_users+'. | Error: Space separator.

cards.q: Scenario: Rotate DB password. Where?
cards.a: Action: Cloud Environment Credentials. | Error: profiles.yml in Git.

cards.q: Scenario: Block PR merge on test fail. How?
cards.a: Action: Required Check in Git provider. | Error: Manual check.

cards.q: Scenario: Run models modified OR tagged 'finance'. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt run --select state:modified tag:finance'. | Error: Comma separator.

cards.q: Scenario: Track GDPR sensitive models. Config?
cards.a: Action: meta: {pii: true} in YAML. | Error: tags: [pii].

cards.q: Scenario: Skip Staging models if exist. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, run always builds. | Error: --skip-if-exists.

cards.q: Scenario: See Lineage of a Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt ls --select source:name+'. | Error: Prefix select.

cards.q: Scenario: PR blocks on Auth error. Cause?
cards.a: Action: CI role lacks CI schema permissions. | Error: Checking dev personal warehouse permissions.

cards.q: Scenario: See tests for Source in docs. Where?
cards.a: Action: Sources section of docs sidebar. | Error: Models section.

cards.q: Scenario: Build all models children of Source. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt build --select source:name+'. | Error: +source:name.

cards.q: Scenario: PR take 30 mins because all models build. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Slim CI (state:modified+). | Error: Manual tagging.

cards.q: Scenario: Run only failed models from previous run. Selection?
cards.a: Action: state:failed. | Error: dbt retry.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to define a 'Revenue' metric that sums a column in your Marts. Where is the best place to define this logic?
cards.a: Action: Define it as a 'measure' within a 'semantic_model' YAML file. | Error: Hardcoding the sum() in a dbt model; the Semantic Layer allows for dynamic aggregation based on different dimensions.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to join 'Orders' and 'Customers' in the Semantic Layer. How do you define the connection?
cards.a: Action: Define 'Entities' in both semantic models and specify their relationship (e.g., primary vs foreign). | Error: Writing a SQL JOIN in a dbt model; the Semantic Layer handles joins dynamically via Entity resolution.

cards.q: Scenario: You need a 'Gross Margin' metric calculated as (Revenue - Cost) / Revenue. Which metric type is this?
cards.a: Action: A 'derived' metric. | Error: A 'simple' metric; simple metrics only perform direct aggregations on single columns.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate 'Cumulative Revenue' over all time. Which metric type should you use?
cards.a: Action: A 'cumulative' metric. | Error: A 'derived' metric; derived metrics are for mathematical operations on other metrics, not for running totals.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing a 'fan-out' error when querying a metric. How do you fix the join logic?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the 'Entity' types are correctly defined as 'primary' or 'unique' to let MetricFlow handle the grain. | Error: Manually adding 'DISTINCT' to the SQL; the Semantic Layer generates the SQL, so you must fix the metadata definition.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to filter a metric to only show 'Completed' orders. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: Define a 'filter' on the metric or use a 'dimension' with a restricted grain. | Error: Creating a separate dbt model called 'fct_completed_orders'; this creates DAG bloat when filters can be handled semantically.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to access metrics from a BI tool that doesn't natively support dbt. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the dbt Semantic Layer JDBC or GraphQL API. | Error: Exporting the metric data to a CSV and uploading it; this breaks the 'Single Source of Truth' benefit.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a metric that calculates 'Average Order Value'. Logic?
cards.a: Action: Define a 'ratio' metric using the Revenue metric as the numerator and the Order Count metric as the denominator. | Error: Using a 'measure' with an 'avg()' aggregation; ratios should be calculated at the end of the aggregation to avoid mathematical errors.

cards.q: Scenario: You changed a column name in your Marts, and now the Semantic Layer is broken. Fix?
cards.a: Action: Update the 'expr' or 'name' mapping in the Semantic Model YAML to match the new warehouse column. | Error: Renaming the metric itself; the metric name used by BI tools should stay consistent even if the underlying column changes.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure that 'LTV' is only queried by the 'Finance' team. Where is this governed?
cards.a: Action: Warehouse-level RBAC or dbt Cloud Group permissions (depending on the Semantic Layer gateway). | Error: Trying to set 'access: private' in the semantic model YAML; private access limits 'ref()' in dbt but doesn't block BI queries.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate 'Daily Active Users'. Which aggregation type?
cards.a: Action: count_distinct. | Error: count; standard count would count sessions or events rather than unique user IDs.

cards.q: Scenario: You need a metric that only looks at data from the last 12 months. Config?
cards.a: Action: Add a 'window' or 'filter' property to the metric definition. | Error: Adding a 'WHERE' clause to the underlying dbt model; this prevents users from ever looking back further than 12 months.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to expose 'Product Category' as a filter in your BI tool via the Semantic Layer. What is it called?
cards.a: Action: A 'dimension'. | Error: A 'measure'; measures are aggregations (numbers), while dimensions are attributes (categories).

cards.q: Scenario: Your Semantic Model points to an Ephemeral model. Will it work?
cards.a: Action: No, Semantic Models must point to physical Tables or Views (Sources/Refs). | Error: Assuming MetricFlow can inject the CTE logic; it requires a physical schema to inspect during query generation.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the SQL generated by the Semantic Layer for a specific query. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'dbt sl query' command in the CLI. | Error: Checking 'target/compiled/'; that only shows the model compilation, not the dynamic MetricFlow SQL generation.

cards.q: Scenario: You have multiple 'Date' columns in a model. Which one does the Semantic Layer use for time-series aggregation?
cards.a: Action: The 'dimension' marked as 'type: time' and designated as the primary time index. | Error: Assuming it uses the first date column it finds; you must explicitly define the time dimension grain.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a metric that is shared across multiple projects in a Mesh. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'Cross-project' refs in your semantic model to point to public models in other projects. | Error: Defining the metric in the Producer project; metrics should generally be defined where they are consumed or in a shared Governance project.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check if your Semantic Layer YAML is valid. Command?
cards.a: Action: 'dbt parse' or 'dbt sl validate'. | Error: 'dbt run'; running the models does not validate the semantic layer metadata.

cards.q: Scenario: A BI tool query is slow. You suspect the Semantic Layer is joining too many tables. Troubleshooting?
cards.a: Action: Check the 'Entities' and 'Join' definitions to ensure you aren't creating a 'star' where a 'snowflake' is more efficient. | Error: Indexing the warehouse tables; indices don't fix poor metadata join paths defined in YAML.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to provide a 'Migration' path for an old metric. Best practice?
cards.a: Action: Use 'Labeling' and 'Descriptions' to mark the old metric as deprecated and point to the new one. | Error: Deleting the old metric immediately; this will cause immediate failures in all connected BI dashboards.

cards.q: Scenario: You want a metric to represent 'Conversion Rate' between signups and purchases. Type?
cards.a: Action: Conversion metric. | Error: Ratio metric; while mathematically a ratio, conversion metrics in MetricFlow have specific properties for user-based funnels.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'alias' a column only for the Semantic Layer, not the warehouse. How?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'name' property for the dimension/measure in YAML while the 'expr' points to the warehouse column. | Error: Aliasing in the dbt model; this forces a change on the physical warehouse table which might break other queries.

cards.q: Scenario: You need to sum the 'Order Amount' but only if 'Tax' is included. How?
cards.a: Action: Define a 'measure' with an 'expr' containing the SQL logic: 'order_amount + tax_amount'. | Error: Creating a 'derived' metric; derived metrics are for metric-to-metric math, not column-to-column math.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Maturity' for a metric (e.g., Experimental). Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Use the 'label' or 'meta' field; the Semantic Layer does not have a native 'maturity' property like Exposures. | Error: Trying to use 'maturity: high' in the metric definition.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see which BI tools are querying which metrics. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Explorer (Metric usage analytics). | Error: Checking warehouse 'Query History'; warehouse logs show the generated SQL, but not the specific 'metric name' or 'BI user'.

cards.q: Scenario: You have a 'User' entity in 3 different projects. How does the Semantic Layer know they are the same?
cards.a: Action: Ensure they all share the same 'Entity Name' and are identified by the same primary key in their respective semantic models. | Error: Assuming dbt 'links' them by column name automatically; you must explicitly define entities.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to provide a human-readable name for a metric (e.g., 'Monthly Recurring Revenue'). Property?
cards.a: Action: label. | Error: name; 'name' is the ID used in code/API, 'label' is what the user sees in the BI UI.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate the 'Total' for a column that is already aggregated in the warehouse. Aggregation type?
cards.a: Action: sum. | Error: average; summing an already aggregated average results in mathematical junk.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Metric not found' in your BI tool. PR check?
cards.a: Action: Ensure the semantic model YAML was merged to the 'Production' branch and a production job has run. | Error: Checking your local dbt IDE; the Semantic Layer API only serves metrics from the designated 'Production' manifest.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a 'Time Window' for a conversion (e.g., within 7 days). Where?
cards.a: Action: In the 'type_params' of a Conversion metric. | Error: Using a standard SQL 'DATEDIFF' in the model; conversion windows are a native feature of the Semantic Layer.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'hide' a dimension from end-users in the BI tool. How?
cards.a: Action: Set 'label: null' or use the BI tool's specific hiding functionality; the Semantic Layer does not have a native 'hidden' toggle yet. | Error: Removing it from YAML; if you remove it, users can't group or filter by it at all.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to use a 'Metric' as a filter for another Metric. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Yes, using a 'derived' metric that incorporates conditional logic. | Error: Assuming simple metrics support 'WHERE metric > 100'; filters usually target dimensions, not other metrics.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate the 'Max' temperature in a day. Aggregation?
cards.a: Action: max. | Error: sum; summing temperature is physically incorrect logic.

cards.q: Scenario: Your semantic model is pointing to a 'Source' instead of a 'Model'. Best practice?
cards.a: Action: Point it to a 'Ref' (model) instead; semantic models should be built on cleaned data, not raw source data. | Error: Building directly on 'Source'; if the raw schema changes, your semantic definitions break.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to check the 'Lineage' of a metric. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Lineage tab, including Semantic Nodes). | Error: 'dbt docs generate'; static docs have limited support for complex MetricFlow lineage.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a 'Business Owner' for a specific metric. Property?
cards.a: Action: owner (within the metric config). | Error: group; groups are for models, whereas metrics have their own owner/metadata properties.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure the 'Semantic Layer' works across both BigQuery and Snowflake. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: Ensure your 'expr' SQL logic is cross-compatible or use dbt macros within the expr. | Error: Hardcoding Snowflake-specific syntax in the YAML 'expr' field.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a metric that is 'The count of unique users who ordered'. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A 'simple' metric based on a 'count_distinct' measure of user_id. | Error: A 'simple' metric based on 'count' of user_id; this would count orders, not unique users.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Version History' for a metric definition. Tool?
cards.a: Action: Git (checking the history of the YAML file). | Error: dbt Explorer; Explorer shows the *current* state of the manifest, not a line-by-line version history of the code.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to apply 'Time Grain' (day, month, year) to a metric. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: Define 'granularities' in the time dimension of the semantic model. | Error: Hardcoding 'date_trunc' in the dbt model; the Semantic Layer should handle truncation dynamically.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to create a 'Secondary Calculation' like Year-over-Year growth. How?
cards.a: Action: Use 'type_params' in the metric definition to specify a 'period_over_period' calculation. | Error: Writing a complex self-join in a dbt model; the Semantic Layer performs these window calculations automatically.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Tags' for metrics for easier searching. Where?
cards.a: Action: In the YAML file under the 'metrics' key. | Error: Assuming metrics inherit tags from the underlying model; they are separate resources in the DAG.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Data Freshness' for a metric. How?
cards.a: Action: Look at the freshness of the 'Source' that feeds the Mart that the metric is built on. | Error: Running 'dbt source freshness' on the metric; freshness only applies to warehouse relations.

cards.q: Scenario: You are seeing 'Aggregation mismatch' when querying a metric. Why?
cards.a: Action: You likely tried to sum an average or perform an invalid operation in a derived metric. | Error: Checking the dbt model; the error is in the YAML 'metric' logic, not the model SQL.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'join' three tables in the Semantic Layer. Relationship path?
cards.a: Action: Ensure there is a chain of Entities (e.g., Orders -> Line Items -> Products). | Error: Trying to join Line Items directly to Orders without defining the join keys in both semantic models.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define a metric that is 'Revenue excluding Tax'. Implementation?
cards.a: Action: A 'simple' metric using a measure with 'expr: revenue - tax'. | Error: A 'derived' metric; use derived metrics for math *between* metrics, not column-level math.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see which 'Model' a metric is built on in dbt Explorer. Where?
cards.a: Action: Select the Metric node and view the 'Dependencies' or 'Semantic Model' reference. | Error: Checking the SQL code; the SQL only shows columns, not the semantic relationship.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate 'Net Promoter Score' (NPS). Logic?
cards.a: Action: A 'derived' metric calculating the difference between the '% Promoters' metric and '% Detractors' metric. | Error: A 'simple' metric; NPS is a complex calculation involving multiple sub-aggregations.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to provide 'Help Text' for a dimension in a BI tool. Property?
cards.a: Action: description. | Error: label; labels are short titles, while descriptions contain the detailed hover-over text.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to run a build and skip 'Semantic Models' to save time. Possible?
cards.a: Action: Semantic models are metadata and aren't 'built' in the warehouse; they are parsed during compilation. | Error: Using '--exclude resource_type:semantic_model' to speed up the warehouse run.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure 'Metric Drift' (logic divergence) doesn't happen. Strategy?
cards.a: Action: Enforce that all BI tools query the Semantic Layer instead of writing their own SQL. | Error: Allowing users to create 'calculated fields' in Tableau that mirror dbt metrics.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate 'Year-to-Date' Revenue. Configuration?
cards.a: Action: A 'cumulative' metric with the 'period: year' parameter. | Error: A 'simple' metric; simple metrics only aggregate the current time slice, not a running history.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Alternative Names' for a metric so users can find it easily. Property?
cards.a: Action: synonyms. | Error: label; a metric only has one label, but can have multiple synonyms for searchability.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see the 'Performance' of Semantic Layer queries. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Cloud Explorer (Performance tab). | Error: Checking dbt Cloud 'Run History'; run history only tracks dbt build/run jobs, not live Semantic Layer API calls.

cards.q: Scenario: You are versioning a semantic model. Syntax?
cards.a: Action: Semantic models do not currently support native versioning (v1, v2) like models. | Error: Attempting to add 'versions:' to the semantic_model YAML block.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to calculate 'Churn Rate'. Metric type?
cards.a: Action: A 'ratio' metric (Lost Customers / Total Customers). | Error: A 'simple' metric; churn requires two separate aggregations to be calculated correctly.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to 'Filter' a semantic model based on a dynamic environment variable. Possible?
cards.a: Action: No, semantic models in YAML must use static filters or dimensions. | Error: Trying to use '{{ env_var(...) }}' inside a semantic model 'expr' or 'filter'.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to define 'Metric Grain' (e.g., by Country). Where?
cards.a: Action: Define 'Country' as a categorical 'dimension' in the semantic model. | Error: Hardcoding a 'GROUP BY country' in the dbt model; MetricFlow handles grouping dynamically.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to see 'Tests' associated with a metric. Tool?
cards.a: Action: dbt Explorer (Metrics tab, showing attached Data Tests). | Error: Assuming metrics can't be tested; you can apply dbt tests to metrics to ensure they return expected values.

cards.q: Scenario: You want to ensure the Semantic Layer stays updated with your dbt project. Requirement?
cards.a: Action: A production job must run successfully to update the 'manifest.json' used by the Semantic API. | Error: Thinking the Semantic Layer updates 'live' as you save YAML files in the IDE.
